Behavior-Based Education | Arts-Based Learning
Introducing CAMEN Academy For The Performing Arts
Arts-Based Learning in Florida
Welcome to the CAMEN Academy for The Performing Arts! Our academy is a specialty school that provides high-quality education to students with unique and nuerodiverse backgrounds in the Orlando area. We use the performing arts as a conduit to facilitate unique learning methods and activities that are suited to the individual needs of the students we have. Our approach emphasizes behavior-based education in Orlando, Florida, aligning instruction with strategies that promote growth through consistent structure and positive reinforcement. We work closely with the student’s guardians to achieve the goals and objectives that the school has. Let’s work hand-in-hand in giving each student the level of education that they deserve.

# Behavior-Based Education | Arts-Based Learning

## Introducing CAMEN Academy For The Performing Arts

## Arts-Based Learning in Florida

Welcome to the CAMEN Academy for The Performing Arts! Our academy is a specialty school that provides high-quality education to students with unique and nuerodiverse backgrounds in the Orlando area. We use the performing arts as a conduit to facilitate unique learning methods and activities that are suited to the individual needs of the students we have. Our approach emphasizes behavior-based education in Orlando, Florida, aligning instruction with strategies that promote growth through consistent structure and positive reinforcement. We work closely with the student’s guardians to achieve the goals and objectives that the school has. Let’s work hand-in-hand in giving each student the level of education that they deserve.

# Charter School | Central Florida Leadership Academy

## CENTRAL FLORIDA LEADERSHIP ACADEMY (CFLA) IS A COLLEGE PREP CHARTER SCHOOL FOR STUDENTS IN GRADES 6-12. OUR MISSION IS TO DEVELOP CIVIC LEADERS DISTINGUISHED BY EXCELLENT CHARACTER.

## PERSONABLE. 

We are a small school by design because we believe students learn best in an environment where every adult knows and greets them by name.

![A female art teacher gives feedback to a female student while a male student listens in as well.](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/c94bd8_90ed2191d68f4902bd5980279d842bd1~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_322,h_209,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/CFLA49.jpg)

## PUBLIC.

Tuition-free, strict academic and financial accountability, and access to a variety of family support services. We bring all the benefits that  a public school offers.

![A male student studying a script in his Theatre class.](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/c94bd8_a0fac2df4ec842929b925c27bcbb0bfa~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_309,h_228,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/studying_edited.jpg)

![A male board member guest speaking in a social studies lesson on the branches of government. ](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/c94bd8_7b2ab60596eb4d8c9358de4b8e280ff5~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_366,h_239,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/100_4387_edited_edited.jpg)

A clear mission, a strong identity, and a holistic approach to education focused on developing civic leaders. We create a focused, high-expectation environment typically associated with costlier private schools.

## 10-1  
FACULTY STUDENT RATIO

## 95%+ GRADUATION RATE SINCE FOUNDING

## ATHLETICS  
&  
PERFORMING ARTS

## AFTER SCHOOL  
CARE  
OFFERED

## COME LEARN. GO LEAD.

We are dedicated to providing a top-tier education without expense to our students. Our educational approach focuses on leadership, college preparation, life preparation, interscholastic athletics, and arts.

​

We aren't just a school.  We are a community for families and students alike to learn and grow together.

![CFLA Volleyball Team at practice](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/c94bd8_3dc6228c08c6469fb9142205c32d2ebb~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_414,h_271,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/stretching_edited.jpg)

![Two students listening to a Social Studies teacher reviewing a presentation.](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/c94bd8_3ced5ccc4401478284e62063561d1bda~mv2.jpg/v1/crop/x_0,y_59,w_3600,h_2282/fill/w_380,h_235,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/CFLA37.jpg)

text:
Center Academy | Special Education Schools in Florida
A Great Place to LearnEverything
Welcome to Center Academy
Center Academy is a fully accredited, independent private school, family-operated since 1968,
providing a college prep curriculum for students with learning differences in grades 4-12.
Recognized as a trusted special education school and private school for special needs,
Center Academy supports students with ADHD and learning disabilities through personalized instruction and small class sizes,
making it a top choice for families searching for learning disability schools or IEP-focused education.

Learn the Best Way.
Center Academy will improve its students’ self-esteem, self-concept, and self-confidence.

We Prioritize Safety.
Center Academy creates an environment where students feel comfortable and safe.

Motivated to Succeed.
Center Academy inspires students to give their best in all tasks they undertake.

Individualized Excellence.
Our staff has a true understanding of the students’ needs and delivers learning with skill and patience.
Students who attend Center Academy have a high graduation rate, gain a sense of self-esteem and personal motivation, experience fewer bullying and peer pressure issues, while attaining a quality education. With small class sizes, and individual attention, the Center Academy school experience gives students many distinct advantages. Our students acquire the abilities which help with success in college and in life.
Center Academy
by Sarah T., 10ᵗʰ Grade Student

Caring for our future
Earning our respect
Never giving up on us
Taking care of us
Encouraging us to do our best
Revolving around our needs
—

Creating a system that is right for us
Acting calm in a hectic situation
Determined to help us graduate
Erasing any flaw that we might have
Making school easier
Yearly dedication

Students who attend Center Academy have a high graduation rate, gain a sense of self-esteem and personal motivation, experience fewer bullying and peer pressure issues, while attaining a quality education. With small class sizes, and individual attention, the Center Academy school experience gives students many distinct advantages. Our students acquire the abilities which help with success in college and in life.

jsonLd.name: Central Florida Preparatory School
jsonLd.description: Central Florida Preparatory School is a private, independent college preparatory school in Apopka, FL serving students from Montessori (age 3) through 12th grade. Founded in 1990.

mainEntity.name: What grades does Central Florida Prep serve?

acceptedAnswer.text: Central Florida Prep serves students from Montessori (ages 3–6) through 12th grade, offering a complete PreK–12 college preparatory education.

mainEntity.name: Where is Central Florida Prep located?

acceptedAnswer.text: CFP is located at 1800 Marden Road, Apopka, FL 32703, on a 5-acre campus with a state-of-the-art facility.
Yes. CFP is accredited by Cognia, MSA CESS (Middle States Association), and is a member of AISF and NCPSA.

mainEntity.name: How do I request a tour at Central Florida Prep?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can request a campus tour by calling (407) 290-8073 or visiting cfprep.org to request a tour online.

# Central Florida Preparatory School | Private PreK-12 Orlando

![Central Florida Prep Campus](https://cfprep.org/images/hero-campus.webp)

## Bringing Innovationto Education

Central Florida Prep is an independent private school in Apopka, Florida  
for students in Pre-Kindergarten through Grade 12.

![](https://cfprep.org/images/school-logo.webp) 

## We believe that innovation belongs in every classroom. That character matters as much as curriculum. And that when you truly know a child, you unlock everything they're capable of.

Pre-K through 12th grade. An average SAT of 1280, 100% college matriculation, and a curriculum designed around the way each child actually learns.

Home of the Mariners. Seven league championships and a program that develops discipline, resilience, and leadership on every court and field.

Theater, studio art, and music programs that cultivate creative confidence and give every student a stage to be seen.

text:
Home | My Site
I will instruct thee and teach thee in the way which thou shalt go: I will guide thee with mine eye.
-Psalm 32:8
The Difference that Lasts a Lifetime
Our purpose is to instill character, foster discipline, and develop integrity in students through Christ-honoring Teaching.
Admissions
Eastland Christian School is currently enrolling students in K4-12th for the 2024/25 school year. We are accredited through FACCS.
academics
Eastland Christian School is a K4-12th grade college-preparatory school. God’s Word is the foundation for ECS curriculum.
Athletics
Our athletic department is dedicated to enhancing the physical, spiritual, moral and social skills of our students.
Events
There are many events that Eastland Christian School Host through-out the year.
Let's Work Together
​
At Eastland Christian School, we believe that working together is the key to building a strong future for our students and our nation. # Faith Christian Academy | Orlando – A Ministry of Faith Assembly

## Academics

We place a strong emphasis on providing a well-rounded education for our students while prioritizing the integration of a biblical worldview into all aspects of our curriculum.

[More Information](https://fcalions.org/explore/academics)

## Athletics

We offer a wide range of sports & athletic program options to allow all students to find a program that interests them while promoting physical and mental health.

[More Information](https://fcalions.org/explore/athletics)

## Creative Arts

Our exceptional professional Creative Arts Program nurtures students creative talents allowing students to explore their interests and discover new passions.

[More Information](https://fcalions.org/explore/arts)

## International Students

FCA offers our international students a unique blend of academic excellence, state-of-the-art facilities, and an enriching cultural experience.

[More Information](https://fcalions.org/explore/international)

## Admissions

Take the next step toward an exceptional education—apply today to join a community where your student’s potential will be nurtured and celebrated.

[More Information](https://fcalions.org/explore/admissions)

## Homeschool

Our Homeschool Program provides support and a system of accountability for parents who desire to educate their children at home.

[More Information](https://fcalions.org/explore/homeschool)

## $2.1 million

in scholarships were awarded to the graduating class of 2022

## 21

Honors and AP Level Courses

## 14

Students were College Board recognized for the 2021-2022 school year

## 81

Students were awarded the Presidential Award

## 4

Student Athletes committed to play intercollegiate athletics in 2022

## Events

![](https://fcalions.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/SummerBreakSpot.png "SummerBreakSpot")

## Free Lunch

Monday through Friday, May 27-July 25  
Curry Ford Campus

Serving free lunch to everyone 18 and under,  
all food must be consumed in the dining hall.

Lunch served: 11:00 am -1:00 pm  
Snack served: 3:00 pm – 4:00 pm  
Closures: 6-19-2025 and 7-4-2025

Park in Lot 5 to enter the FCA Main Office

text:
Christian School Orlando Florida | College Prep School | Heritage Prep | Independent Baptist School | K-12 West Orlando | Private School Pine Hills | Ocoee Orlo Vista
Welcome to
Heritage Preparatory School
Providing a quality Christian education in Orlando, Florida for over 50 years.
Heritage Preparatory School was founded in 1969 to provide spiritual, academic, athletic and fine arts training for students in Orange County, Lake County, Osceola, Seminole and all of Central Florida. Heritage also has I-20 capability and educates international students from South America, Korea, China and the Caribbean.
We are an accredited Christian school with a small average class size of only 15 students and 90 percent of Heritage graduates continue into higher learning. Heritage offers a K-12 college preparatory, Bible-based education that meets Florida qualification for the Bright Futures Scholarships. We are competitive in several sports through the Florida Christian Conference. Call today and discover the benefits of one of the best Christian Schools in Orlando.
Integrating Faith & Learning
Heritage Preparatory School offers a demanding academic program with a distinctively Christian character for boys and girls. It is a place where you can feel confident that the principles of respect, honesty, community, creativity and faith that you have instilled at home are reinforced on a daily basis. Heritage Preparatory School is a place where your children can feel confident that they are receiving the best possible preparation for college and life, while enjoying the most important years of their development.
For over forty years, Heritage Preparatory School has brought out the best in its students. We have achieved this by offering an education with a clear purpose: an excellent program of learning set in the context of a Christian world and life view.
Here the atmosphere, opportunity, quality of the curriculum, diversity of students and philosophies all combine to distinguish us from other public and private independent schools.
Tabernacle Baptist Church
Our parent church has grown from one small building into a facility of 62,000 square feet with a Bible believing Church, and a Christian school dedicated to quality academics and spiritual growth.
Visit our church website
Body, Mind & Spirit
God has placed the very important responsibility of training children in the hands of parents. It is a critical time to permanently affect the hearts and minds of children in their earliest years.
Heritage Preparatory School can assist parents with this training process. The purpose of Heritage Preparatory School is to incorporate a student’s studies with a relationship with God and family values. Working with the home, Heritage Preparatory School provides a safe and loving environment in which to learn and grow.
Heritage Preparatory School is a Christian School commissioned to provide high quality and affordable education which ministers to the whole child -- body, mind and spirit. This preschool through twelfth grade educational experience is designed to reinforce the values and morals taught in the Christian home and church. Highly trained and experienced teachers demonstrate God’s love through their commitment to students while encouraging excellence and touching hearts, both for now and the future.
“We want our students– whatever their plans for the future– to take from high school a shared body of knowledge and skills, a common language of ideas, a common moral and intellectual discipline. We want them to know math and science, history and literature."
"We want them to know how to think for themselves, to respond to important questions, to solve problems, to pursue an argument, to defend a point of view, to understand its opposite, and weigh the alternatives."
"We want them to develop, through example and experience, those habits of mind and traits of character properly prized by our society. And we want them to be prepared for entry into the community of adults.”
William J. Bennett
Former U.S. Secretary of Education
Here’s why Heritage Prep is a better choice for your children...
40 years of education experience
Smaller classes; average less than 15 students
Accredited through NAC and AFCI
Heritage meets Florida qualification for Bright Futures Scholarships
90% of Heritage graduates continue to higher learning
Heritage integrates faith and education
Qualifying students receive free tutoring in reading, math and study skills
Heritage ministers to the whole student; body, mind and spirit
Philosophy
The philosophy of Heritage Schools is that the school should cooperate fully with the home and the church to develop each individual to his fullest potential encompassing these qualities:
Relationship with God
Responsible and productive citizenship
Growth in skills and knowledge
Physical and emotional well-being
Moral conduct in all relationships
Development of social skills and good manners
The Bible is presented as the inspired Word of God and the Lord Jesus Christ as the solution to the problems of our day.
Mission Statement &
Standards of Ethical Conduct
Heritage Preparatory School was founded in 1969 to provide spiritual, academic, athletic and fine arts training for Christian young people in Orange County and adjacent counties.
Read the Heritage Standards of Ethical Conduct

text:
Innovation Montessori Ocoee | Montessori School
FAST FACTS
Our Curriculum
We strive to provide quality instruction which holds a strong commitment to the founding principles and philosophies of the Montessori Method and inspires academic excellence in an environment rich with warmth, kindness, respect; nurturing curiosity, creativity, and independence.
Our Location
We are located in the heart of Ocoee and serve students throughout Central Florida including Winter Garden, Windermere, Apopka, and more.
Our Staff
Our lead teachers, in every classroom, have at least a bachelor’s degree, Florida Department of Education teacher certification, and have a certification training from a MACTE-accredited Montessori teacher training centers.
Our History
We started as Montessori of Winter Garden Charter in 2011 in a strip mall in Winter Garden, Florida. Since then, we have grown into a two-campus Orange County Charter School serving students from the age of 3 to 12th grade. We now call Ocoee home.
Our Charter
Innovation Montessori of Ocoee is an Orange County Public Charter School. A lottery is held yearly with priority given to Orange County students.
Our Goal
Our Goal is to provide a strong Montessori foundation that enables our students to achieve their full potential, develop curiosity, creativity, imagination, and become responsible citizens of the world.
A Welcome from our Executive Director
markdown:
# Innovation Montessori Ocoee | Montessori School

## FAST FACTS

*   ## Our Curriculum
    

We strive to provide quality instruction which holds a strong commitment to the founding principles and philosophies of the Montessori Method and inspires academic excellence in an environment rich with warmth, kindness, respect; nurturing curiosity, creativity, and independence.

*   ## Our Location
    

We are located in the heart of Ocoee and serve students throughout Central Florida including Winter Garden, Windermere, Apopka, and more.

*   ## Our Staff
    

Our lead teachers, in every classroom, have at least a bachelor’s degree, Florida Department of Education teacher certification, and have a certification training from a MACTE-accredited Montessori teacher training centers.

*   ## Our History
    

We started as Montessori of Winter Garden Charter in 2011 in a strip mall in Winter Garden, Florida. Since then, we have grown into a two-campus Orange County Charter School serving students from the age of 3 to 12th grade. We now call Ocoee home.

*   ## Our Charter
    

Innovation Montessori of Ocoee is an Orange County Public Charter School. A lottery is held yearly with priority given to Orange County students.

*   ## Our Goal
    

Our Goal is to provide a strong Montessori foundation that enables our students to achieve their full potential, develop curiosity, creativity, imagination, and become responsible citizens of the world.

## A Welcome from our Executive Director

text:
Gotlib Jewish Academy
Hero Shuffle (redesign)
The Premier Elementary School Education in Central Florida.
The Gotlib Jewish Academy develops strong leaders and critical thinkers by providing the highest educational standards in an individualized learning environment. We celebrate our Jewish identity, heritage and values while fostering a passion for learning and high achievement. Our students are empowered to achieve academic success, thus becoming role models in our community and the world. 
In 2015-2016 we created our version of a maker space; The Innovation Lab. We utilize the ilab to augment our S.T.E.A.M. infused curriculum, providing technology focussed enrichment opportunities and maker-based activities. All grade levels come to the iLab on a rotating weekly schedule. Classroom teachers also sign up to use the iLab for classroom projects during open times. The iLab provides a safe space for students to fail while supporting inquiry-based learning. Students are able to tinker, experiment and prototype in a safe and supportive environment. Collaboration and teamwork are also strongly encouraged. iLab activities are designed to foster self-reliance, attention to detail, patience, communication and empathy.
At GJA, our approach to focus on the whole child is a core value. We foster the values of kindness, self-awareness, and character within our students, nurturing them to be mindful critical thinkers.
The Gotlib Jewish Academy is also affiliated with PRIZMA - Jewish Community Day School Network. Prizmah strengthens and sustains the Jewish life, leadership and learning of community day schools through out the United States. Prizmah: Center for Jewish Day Schools was founded in 2016, with a mission to transform the North American Jewish day school landscape. Prizmah provides day schools with tools to foster the educational excellence, financial vitality, and community support that will make day school education the first choice for Jewish families. 
From Kindergarten through fifth grade, we, at Gotlib Jewish Academy, are committed to Academic Excellence by kindling a love of learning, a passion for the arts and empowering our students to challenge themselves to go out and change the world!
Honoring Charlotte & Winston Schwartz and Penny Goldstein 
The differentiated learning is a key element to providing a superior education to each and every student. Every child is challenged at their own level in several subjects which allows them to thrive in an environment nurturing individuality, rather than expecting the same standard regardless of the individual. The faculty has immense knowledge in a variety of learning styles to better meet the individual needs of each student. They truly get to know each student and understand how to best support each and every one. We know that the students at JAO are getting the best of the best educators and I only wish that all children were so lucky. The guidance and care and expertise that goes into each day is exceptional. I can’t state enough how beyond satisfied we have been with our experience at JAO. Our middle schooler even comes for Shabbat before her own school begins on most Fridays. It is a truly magical place that will forever reside in the heart of every JAO family.

# Gotlib Jewish Academy

## Hero Shuffle (redesign)

![](https://3.files.edl.io/03c4/23/11/22/192232-b2a4883a-56c3-4ad9-99d7-59bdfcc74683.jpg)

The Premier Elementary School Education in Central Florida.

The Gotlib Jewish Academy develops strong leaders and critical thinkers by providing the highest educational standards in an individualized learning environment. We celebrate our Jewish identity, heritage and values while fostering a passion for learning and high achievement. Our students are empowered to achieve academic success, thus becoming role models in our community and the world. 

 

In 2015-2016 we created our version of a maker space; The Innovation Lab. We utilize the ilab to augment our S.T.E.A.M. infused curriculum, providing technology focussed enrichment opportunities and maker-based activities.  All grade levels come to the iLab on a rotating weekly schedule. Classroom teachers also sign up to use the iLab for classroom projects during open times. The iLab provides a safe space for students to fail while supporting inquiry-based learning. Students are able to tinker, experiment and prototype in a safe and supportive environment. Collaboration and teamwork are also strongly encouraged. iLab activities are designed to foster self-reliance, attention to detail, patience, communication and empathy.

At GJA, our approach to focus on the whole child is a core value.  We foster the values of kindness, self-awareness, and character within our students, nurturing them to be mindful critical thinkers.

The Gotlib Jewish Academy is also affiliated with PRIZMA - Jewish Community Day School Network. Prizmah strengthens and sustains the Jewish life, leadership and learning of community day schools through out the United States. Prizmah: Center for Jewish Day Schools was founded in 2016, with a mission to transform the North American Jewish day school landscape. Prizmah provides day schools with tools to foster the educational excellence, financial vitality, and community support that will make day school education the first choice for Jewish families. 

From Kindergarten through fifth grade, we, at Gotlib Jewish Academy, are committed to Academic Excellence by kindling a love of learning, a passion for the arts and empowering our students to challenge themselves to go out and change the world!

![48th Annual Pearls of Wisdom Gala Featured Photo](https://3.files.edl.io/ac41/25/01/20/231700-c8cc2b18-3897-4723-922c-7e680a61f821.jpg)

Honoring Charlotte & Winston Schwartz and Penny Goldstein

The differentiated learning is a key element to providing a superior education to each and every student. Every child is challenged at their own level in several subjects which allows them to thrive in an environment nurturing individuality, rather than expecting the same standard regardless of the individual. The faculty has immense knowledge in a variety of learning styles to better meet the individual needs of each student. They truly get to know each student and understand how to best support each and every one. We know that the students at JAO are getting the best of the best educators and I only wish that all children were so lucky. The guidance and care and expertise that goes into each day is exceptional. I can’t state enough how beyond satisfied we have been with our experience at JAO. Our middle schooler even comes for Shabbat before her own school begins on most Fridays. It is a truly magical place that will forever reside in the heart of every JAO family.

text:
Keystone Academy
Welcome to Keystone Academy
Keystone Academy is an organization that provides homeschooled students from kindergarten through twelfth grade with weekly academic classes, carefully constructed curriculum, and group enrichment activities. Located in the greater Orlando area, Keystone Academy gives students an opportunity to experience both the benefits of a classroom environment and home study. Experienced teachers instruct students in main concepts on the designated class day, then provide parents with full, classically-inspired curriculum and resources for the remainder of the week. Our goal is to walk alongside homeschool parents offering guidance, encouragement, and community as they progress through their homeschool journey.
markdown:
# Keystone Academy

## Welcome to Keystone Academy

Keystone Academy is an organization that provides homeschooled students from kindergarten through twelfth grade with weekly academic classes, carefully constructed curriculum, and group enrichment activities. Located in the greater Orlando area, Keystone Academy gives students an opportunity to experience both the benefits of a classroom environment and home study. Experienced teachers instruct students in main concepts on the designated class day, then provide parents with full, classically-inspired curriculum and resources for the remainder of the week. Our goal is to walk alongside homeschool parents offering guidance, encouragement, and community as they progress through their homeschool journey.

Orlando Private School | Lake Forrest Preparatory School
challenging minds, At Lake Forrest Preparatory School, each child reaches his or her greatest
potential as a responsible, productive, and capable individual. Nurturing growth, LEARN MORE enriching lives. 
A solid foundation
Lake Forrest Preparatory School provides the choice of an Orlando private school community to a broader population; to create a community school where each child is safe and well-educated.
At Lake Forrest, we believe in partnering with parents to support their children’s education. From preschool through middle school, we emphasize both academic excellence and strong character development. Our curriculum includes character education from the start, helping students grow into confident, values-driven individuals. When they leave Lake Forrest, they have both a solid academic foundation and the strong moral compass needed for success.

Mission
At Lake Forrest Preparatory School, each child reaches his or her greatest potential as a responsible, productive, and capable individual.
Vision
Lake Forrest Preparatory School aspires to create an atmosphere where school is an extension of the family: a school environment where teacher, parent, and student interactions involve solid connections, enriching lessons, and responsibility on the part of all three participants. Throughout preschool, elementary, and middle school, LFPS instills healthy choices and character education.
Values
Lake Forrest Preparatory School is committed to values that support academic excellence and positive interactions among individuals and communities.
We Value:
Character education: responsibility, fairness, citizenship, caring, trustworthiness, and respect
Curriculum that is enriching and relevant; curriculum that is accelerated, but capable of appropriate modifications
Open communication with staff and families including mutual respect for each participant
Student contributions across all academic, social, and community involvement
Diversity in our staff and families
Regular instruction in physical education, music, and Spanish
Responsibility of our environment: recycling, “Big Help”-community service projects
Wearing a uniform that establishes unity and pride in all students
testing
LFPS administers the Educational Development Series (EDSeries) standardized testing for achievement testing at the elementary and middle school levels. We use the most current version available with the newest norms and most updated information when comparing our Orlando private school students to other students at the same age and grade level across the nation, not just in the state of Florida.
We do not put pressure on the students or staff to prepare for this test. The staff is provided with the carefully selected curriculum and any materials necessary to implement the curriculum. Research shows that if the curriculum is taught with integrity by effective teachers, the students will perform to their highest potential.
Achievement testing does not have to be high pressure if everything else is RIGHT.
Individual student test results are reviewed and discussed at parent-teacher conferences at the end of the school year.

markdown:
# Orlando Private School | Lake Forrest Preparatory School

challenging minds, At Lake Forrest Preparatory School, each child reaches his or her greatest  
potential as a responsible, productive, and capable individual. Nurturing growth,  ![](https://www.lakeforrestprep.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/01/LFPS-slider-copy-01.png)[LEARN MORE](https://www.lakeforrestprep.com/admissions) enriching lives.

#### A solid foundation

###### Lake Forrest Preparatory School provides the choice of an Orlando private school community to a broader population; to create a community school where each child is safe and well-educated.

At Lake Forrest, we believe in partnering with parents to support their children’s education. From preschool through middle school, we emphasize both academic excellence and strong character development. Our curriculum includes character education from the start, helping students grow into confident, values-driven individuals. When they leave Lake Forrest, they have both a solid academic foundation and the strong moral compass needed for success.

[more about us](https://lakeforrestprep.com/about/)

![30 Years Sticker](https://www.lakeforrestprep.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/12/30-Years-Sticker-400x400.png "30 Years Sticker")

###### Founded in 1991 by Karen Anderson, LFPS has become a warm and nurturing place that fosters the education and growth of young minds.

[More about our Founder](https://lakeforrestprep.com/our-founder)

#### Age Groups

###### We aspire to create an atmosphere where school is an extension of the family, and where teacher, parent, and student interactions involved solid connections and enriching lessons.

![](https://www.lakeforrestprep.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/LFPS_1264.jpg "LFPS_1264")

#### Preschool

#### 6 weeks – 4 years

Independence, problem solving skills, decision making skills, and creativity are the foundation of learning in the early years.

[Preschool information](https://lakeforrestprep.com/preschool-2/)

![](https://www.lakeforrestprep.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/LFPS_Middle.jpg "LFPS_Elementary")

#### Elementary school

#### Kindergarten – 4th grade

These grades include a diverse curriculum of reading, writing, grammar, social science, math, handwriting, and other enrichment courses.

[Elementary information](https://lakeforrestprep.com/elementary/)

![](https://www.lakeforrestprep.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/12/LFPS_middleschool-1.jpg "LFPS_middleschool")

#### Middle school

#### 5th – 8th grade

These grades feature more advanced curriculum including literature, writing, math, science, social studies, and other electives.

[Middle school information](https://lakeforrestprep.com/middle-school/)

#### Mission

###### At Lake Forrest Preparatory School, each child reaches his or her greatest potential as a responsible, productive, and capable individual.

#### [Vision](https://www.lakeforrestprep.com/#1498475487619-7bed29a2-3ad3)

Lake Forrest Preparatory School aspires to create an atmosphere where school is an extension of the family: a school environment where teacher, parent, and student interactions involve solid connections, enriching lessons, and responsibility on the part of all three participants. Throughout preschool, elementary, and middle school, LFPS instills healthy choices and character education.

#### [Values](https://www.lakeforrestprep.com/#1498475487664-906741ba-4d09)

Lake Forrest Preparatory School is committed to values that support academic excellence and positive interactions among individuals and communities.

**We Value:**

*   *   Character education: responsibility, fairness, citizenship, caring, trustworthiness, and respect
    *   Curriculum that is enriching and relevant; curriculum that is accelerated, but capable of appropriate modifications
    *   Open communication with staff and families including mutual respect for each participant
    *   Student contributions across all academic, social, and community involvement
    *   Diversity in our staff and families
    *   Regular instruction in physical education, music, and Spanish
    *   Responsibility of our environment: recycling, “Big Help”-community service projects
    *   Wearing a uniform that establishes unity and pride in all students

#### [testing](https://www.lakeforrestprep.com/#1498475519147-80e89ff1-97ad)

LFPS administers the Educational Development Series (EDSeries) standardized testing for achievement testing at the elementary and middle school levels. We use the most current version available with the newest norms and most updated information when comparing our **Orlando private school** students to other students at the same age and grade level across the nation, not just in the state of Florida.

We do not put pressure on the students or staff to prepare for this test. The staff is provided with the carefully selected curriculum and any materials necessary to implement the curriculum. Research shows that if the curriculum is taught with integrity by effective teachers, the students will perform to their highest potential.

Achievement testing does not have to be high pressure if everything else is RIGHT.

Individual student test results are reviewed and discussed at parent-teacher conferences at the end of the school year.

*   [Vision](https://www.lakeforrestprep.com/#1498475487619-7bed29a2-3ad3)
*   [Values](https://www.lakeforrestprep.com/#1498475487664-906741ba-4d09)
*   [testing](https://www.lakeforrestprep.com/#1498475519147-80e89ff1-97ad)

**VICE PRINCIPAL AND ADMISSIONS**

text:
About Us - Aspire Charter AcademyAspire Charter Academy
Aspire Charter Academy is considered a Model Orange County Charter Elementary School for our specialized focus on positive behaviors and activities that uplift young minds and spirits. We are determined to create and foster a ‘family’ atmosphere so that every child can realize their full potential.
Aspire Charter Academy has an open enrollment of no more than 140 students. Aspire’s enrollment is open so long as there is space available in the classrooms.
Leadership
Aspire Charter Academy was founded by Sheree' Fox and Pamela Schenkel. They created our school to serve students and families in our community that are at risk of being lost in larger schools. Our school is intentionally small so that we can build relationships with our students and families to provide them with a great school experience.
Since the retirement of our founders, we have acquired an Administrative team that continues and builds on the foundation they laid. They share the founding vision, continue to build relationships with students and families to enrich the learning experience and build invaluable partnerships to help connect our families to local resources.
Last, but certainly not least, our school would not be what it is now without our fantastic team of teachers and support staff. We cannot emphasize enough the effect they have on our students and their futures. We are so honored they choose to work with us.
Parent Liaison/Parental Involvement Contact
Shanda Fox & Jodi Shea
Thomas Durkee, President
Sharon Owens
Marjorie Moreland
Elaine Fitzgerald
Dave Herbster
markdown:
# About Us - Aspire Charter AcademyAspire Charter Academy

Aspire Charter Academy is considered a Model Orange County Charter Elementary School for our specialized focus on positive behaviors and activities that uplift young minds and spirits. We are determined to create and foster a ‘family’ atmosphere so that every child can realize their full potential.

Aspire Charter Academy has an open enrollment of no more than 140 students. Aspire’s enrollment is open so long as there is space available in the classrooms.

![Pamela Schenkel](https://aspirecharteracademy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/jshea-scaled.png "Shanda Fox & Jodi Shea")

**Leadership**

Aspire Charter Academy was founded by Sheree' Fox and Pamela Schenkel. They created our school to serve students and families in our community that are at risk of being lost in larger schools.  Our school is intentionally small so that we can build relationships with our students and families to provide them with a great school experience.

Since the retirement of our founders, we have acquired an Administrative team that continues and builds on the foundation they laid.  They share the founding vision, continue to build relationships with students and families to enrich the learning experience and build invaluable partnerships to help connect our families to local resources.

Last, but certainly not least, our school would not be what it is now without our fantastic team of teachers and support staff.  We cannot emphasize enough the effect they have on our students and their futures. We are so honored they choose to work with us.

### Parent Liaison/Parental Involvement Contact

Shanda Fox & Jodi Shea

Thomas Durkee, President  
Sharon Owens  
Marjorie Moreland  
Elaine Fitzgerald  
Dave Herbster

text:
Enrollment / Parent Contact - Aspire Charter AcademyAspire Charter Academy
Open Enrollment Policy
Aspire Charter Academy has the capacity to serve 140 students. Aspire Charter Academy reserves the right to close enrollment when it cannot serve students effectively, due to individual needs of specific students. The Controlled Open Enrollment Plan is as follows:
Aspire Charter Academy is accepting applications for the 2023 – 2024 school year. Applicants may start the application process by using the form provided on this site.
Once your inquiry is received, a member of the Administrative team will contact you to schedule an interview and request further information.
We will continue to take applications until our classes are full. Applicants applying after classes are full will be added to a waiting list if placement options are not available. If you have any questions, please contact Shanda Fox (Director/Parent Contact) at 407-297-9955.
markdown:
# Enrollment / Parent Contact - Aspire Charter AcademyAspire Charter Academy

**Open Enrollment Policy**

Aspire Charter Academy has the capacity to serve 140 students. Aspire Charter Academy reserves the right to close enrollment when it cannot serve students effectively, due to individual needs of specific students. The Controlled Open Enrollment Plan is as follows:

Aspire Charter Academy is accepting applications for the 2023 – 2024 school year. Applicants may start the application process by using the form provided on this site.

Once your inquiry is received, a member of the Administrative team will contact you to schedule an interview and request further information.

We will continue to take applications until our classes are full. Applicants applying after classes are full will be added to a waiting list if placement options are not available. If you have any questions, please contact Shanda Fox (Director/Parent Contact) at [407-297-9955](tel:407-297-9955).

text:
Aspire Charter AcademyAspire Charter Academy
There is no one-size-fits-all educational solution for children. That’s why we created Aspire Charter Academy, a Tuition-Free Public School for children in grades K – 5, who live in Orange County and surrounding counties. Aspire Charter Academy is based on the principles of Positive Behavior Support. We believe that children can and will respond in responsible ways when their efforts are met with recognition and reward.
Services
Our goal is to make sure every child in Orange County, in grades K – 5, has the chance to succeed at their best in a supportive environment. In addition to unique tuition-free classroom experiences, Aspire Charter Academy offers these no-cost services for qualified students:
Rigorous General Education Curriculum
Special Education Services 
Speech-Language Therapy
Occupational Therapy
Small Group Instruction
Specialized Instructional Techniques
Integrated Language Support
Individualized Behavior Intervention Plans
School-wide Positive Behavior Support
All students attending are provided free breakfast and lunch. Transportation is available for select areas of Orlando.
markdown:
# Aspire Charter AcademyAspire Charter Academy

There is no one-size-fits-all educational solution for children. That’s why we created Aspire Charter Academy, a Tuition-Free Public School for children in grades K – 5, who live in Orange County and surrounding counties. Aspire Charter Academy is based on the principles of Positive Behavior Support. We believe that children can and will respond in responsible ways when their efforts are met with recognition and reward.

**Services**

Our goal is to make sure every child in Orange County, in grades K – 5, has the chance to succeed at their best in a supportive environment. In addition to unique tuition-free classroom experiences, Aspire Charter Academy offers these no-cost services for qualified students:

*   Rigorous General Education Curriculum
*   Special Education Services
    *   Speech-Language Therapy
    *   Occupational Therapy
    *   Small Group Instruction
    *   Specialized Instructional Techniques
    *   Integrated Language Support
    *   Individualized Behavior Intervention Plans
    *   School-wide Positive Behavior Support

All students attending are provided free breakfast and lunch.   Transportation is available for select areas of Orlando.

# Behavioral Health Care in Florida

![Speech training for kids](https://www.wearecamen.com/wp-content/themes/camenbehavez311/intro_template/images/intro_2139736355.jpg)

Camen Behavioral Services' mission is to provide personalized, high-quality behavior analysis services. We strive to provide ABA with compassion, and we have created a practice that we believe in.

[Camen Behavioral](https://www.camenbehavioral.com/home)

![Pupils working at their desks](https://www.wearecamen.com/wp-content/themes/camenbehavez311/intro_template/images/intro_309241193.jpg)

[Camen Academy](https://www.camenacademy.com/)

[Camen University](https://www.camenbehavioral.com/applied-behavior-analysis-services/rbt-course)

![Farmer's hand planting a seed in soil](https://www.wearecamen.com/wp-content/themes/camenbehavez311/intro_template/images/intro_550823812.jpg)

## Foundation

We strive to give individuals with varying exceptionalities opportunities to excel.

[Camen Foundation](https://www.camenfoundation.org/)

text:
About | Central Florida Leadership Academy
The mission of the Central Florida Leadership Academy (CFLA) is to develop civic leaders distinguished by excellent character.
How do we do that? 
By providing college preparatory academics in combination with after-school care, performing arts and athletics programs and family-based, character education in an environment that changes attitudes and develops leaders, enabling each student to achieve his or her best. 
Our school is modeled after the highly successful Hyde Schools, which have been the national leaders in family based character education for more than 50 years.
markdown:
# About | Central Florida Leadership Academy

The mission of the Central Florida Leadership Academy (CFLA) is to develop civic leaders distinguished by excellent character.

How do we do that?

By providing college preparatory academics in combination with after-school care, performing arts and athletics programs and family-based, character education in an environment that changes attitudes and develops leaders, enabling each student to achieve his or her best.

Our school is modeled after the highly successful [Hyde Schools](https://www.hyde.edu/), which have been the national leaders in family based character education for more than 50 years.

metadata.title: Academics | CFLAcademy
metadata.description:
Central Florida Leadership Academy (CFLA) emphasizes college preparation and student responsibility, with over 95% of graduates expected to attend four-year colleges. Based on the Hyde School model, it focuses on character development and academic mastery.
Middle School: 132-student capacity with core subjects and electives (Art, Music, Performing Arts).
High School: 175-student capacity, offering college preparatory classes, online advancement options, and aims for a 100% graduation rate.

High School: 175-student capacity, offering college preparatory classes, online advancement options, and aims for a 100% graduation rate.

text:
Academics | CFLAcademy
ACADEMICS
Central Florida Leadership Academy offers college prep academics, with students taking primary responsibility for their learning. More than 95 percent of CFLA graduates are expected to ultimately gain acceptance at four-year colleges (some following completion of a two-year college program), consistent with the schools on which CFLA is modeled.
The CFLA approach to education is based on the Hyde School model, which has been developed since 1966 at the Hyde boarding campuses in Bath, ME, and Woodstock, CT, and through public school initiatives in New Haven, CT, and Washington, DC. CFLA students are expected to master the Next Generation Sunshine State Educational Standards, with character development at the heart of the educational experience.
CFLA offers seven class periods each day, allowing students additional time in class and flexibility in selecting electives of their choice.
Middle School
Capacity: 132 students
In addition to core subjects, CFLA offers middle school electives such as Art, Music, Performing Arts, Languages, Computers, Reading, Intensive Math, Peer Counseling and Leadership Skills, Speech and Debate, Creative Writing and Physical Education.
High School
Capacity: 175 students
CFLA offers all classes required for a four year college preparatory diploma. Students are also provided the opportunity to take on-line classes to advance their academics or explore areas of interest. A 100% high school graduation rate is expected each year.

markdown:
# Academics | CFLAcademy

##### ACADEMICS

Central Florida Leadership Academy offers college prep academics, with students taking primary responsibility for their learning. More than 95 percent of CFLA graduates are expected to ultimately gain acceptance at four-year colleges (some following completion of a two-year college program), consistent with the schools on which CFLA is modeled.

![CFLA STUDENTS LEARNING TOGETHER](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/c94bd8_b1a0ea8a13a94626a589e2b65fdc4869~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_459,h_340,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/groupwork.jpg)

The CFLA approach to education is based on the Hyde School model, which has been developed since 1966 at the Hyde boarding campuses in Bath, ME, and Woodstock, CT, and through public school initiatives in New Haven, CT, and Washington, DC. CFLA students are expected to master the Next Generation Sunshine State Educational Standards, with character development at the heart of the educational experience.

CFLA offers seven class periods each day, allowing students additional time in class and flexibility in selecting electives of their choice.

## Middle School

Capacity: 132 students

In addition to core subjects, CFLA offers middle school electives such as Art, Music, Performing Arts, Languages, Computers, Reading, Intensive Math, Peer Counseling and Leadership Skills, Speech and Debate, Creative Writing and Physical Education.

## High School

Capacity: 175 students

 CFLA offers all classes required for a four year college preparatory diploma. Students are also provided the opportunity to take on-line classes to advance their academics or explore areas of interest. A 100% high school graduation rate is expected each year.

## 2025 - 2026  
School Schedule & Calendar

Bell Schedule

​​

Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, and Friday  8:10AM - 3:15PM

​

Wednesday

  8:10AM - 2:15PM

​​

​

​Before School Care

​

Monday through Friday

7:00AM-7:45AM

​

After School Care

Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, and Friday 3:15PM - 5:45PM

​

Wednesday 

2:15PM - 5:45PM

text:
Admissions | CFLAcademy
Tours of the school are available by appointment. Applicants will be accepted as space is available. Once a student’s enrollment form has been received, the student and his or her family will be interviewed to ensure that the goals and objectives of CFLA are clearly understood and the family is confident that CFLA is the right choice.
​
A lottery will be held if new applications exceed available spaces in any grade level. Current students and their siblings are given priority in enrollment.
First time enrolled students will be asked to attend an orientation program in the summer the week prior to the beginning of school.
CFLA does not discriminate in admission or access to, or treatment or employment in its programs and activities on the basis of race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, marital status, disability or any other reason prohibited by law.
Mid-term enrollment may be available if space permits.
​
Please contact us directly at 407.480.2352 or by email at enroll@cflacademy.org.
markdown:
# Admissions | CFLAcademy

Tours of the school are available by appointment. Applicants will be accepted as space is available.  Once a student’s enrollment form has been received, the student and his or her family will be interviewed to ensure that the goals and objectives of CFLA are clearly understood and the family is confident that CFLA is the right choice.

​

A lottery will be held if new applications exceed available spaces in any grade level. Current students and their siblings are given priority in enrollment.

First time enrolled students will be asked to attend an orientation program in the summer the week prior to the beginning of school.

CFLA does not discriminate in admission or access to, or treatment or employment in its programs and activities on the basis of race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, marital status, disability or any other reason prohibited by law.

Mid-term enrollment may be available if space permits.

​

Please contact us directly at 407.480.2352 or by email at [](mailto:enroll@cflacademy.org)[enroll@cflacademy.org](mailto:enroll@cflacademy.org).

What Customers Are Saying
“Gradelink makes school management easy!”
—Debbie S., Academic Guidance
“Best tool for teachers and parents. I can rely on Gradelink for my grades, communication, and posting information and links. I have been using it for nine years now, and it just gets better every year. I cannot imagine teaching without it.”
—Cathy
“We all love using Gradelink! As a teacher, my efficiency has improved tremendously. Students are also adept at monitoring their grades.”
—Robert

—Reviewer on Sourceforge

markdown:
# Gradelink Student Information System: Simplify and Succeed

## What Customers Are Saying

“Gradelink makes school management easy!”

_—Debbie S., Academic Guidance  
_

“Best tool for teachers and parents. I can rely on Gradelink for my grades, communication, and posting information and links. I have been using it for nine years now, and it just gets better every year. I cannot imagine teaching without it.”

_—Cathy  
_

“We all love using Gradelink! As a teacher, my efficiency has improved tremendously. Students are also adept at monitoring their grades.”

_—Robert  
_

“I have used other school management software, and Gradelink surpasses them all!”

_—Reviewer on Sourceforge  
_

“Best software and customer service indeed. Gradelink is easy to use, with minimal training needed for parents and teachers. Set-up is a breeze and the BEST part is the near-instant customer service that is available through their website, via email, or over the phone.”

_—David L.  
_

“I love Gradelink because it is an all-in-one program! My attendance, lunch count, IEP, and student files are all in one place.”

_—Angela T., Assistant Principal  
_

“Gradelink has made it much easier to enter and keep student grades organized. It is effortless to enter assignment descriptions and adjust the weight of that assignment. I recommend it for a stress-free life.”

_—Mark W., Music Instructor

About Center Academy | Special Ed Schools in Florida
Recent Posts
Student Spotlight
Center Academy Founded in 1968
Special Ed School in Florida
Center Academy began in 1968 with the vision of its founder,
Dr. Mack Hicks, who saw the need for a specialized,
full-day school for students who were falling through the cracks in traditional schools.
Independent Special Education School
—
Center Academy is a private school offering specialized classes for middle-and high-school students with learning differences. Our small classes allow our teachers to accommodate each student’s unique learning style.
Our Teachers Understand and Care
—
At Center Academy, we begin each student’s education with an assessment of their learning style, their personality, and their previous experiences. Our teachers understand the nuances of a wide variety of learning differences such as ADHD, Dyslexia and high-functioning Autism Spectrum Disorder/Asperger’s and work with each student to develop goals that are in line with their unique potential. We provide a caring, comfortable atmosphere, where each student is able to work toward their goals at their own pace, either in a small group setting, or with individualized instruction.
Arrange for a Personal Tour of Center Academy
—
A personal tour of Center Academy will show you why students love it here. We’d love to show you around our campus and introduce you to our staff. We can also review your child’s particular needs, discuss our admissions process, and see whether your child might qualify for one of many scholarship programs. Please call your nearest campus today to arrange a tour!
Contact Us For More Information

## Center Academy Founded in 1968

### Special Ed School in Florida

![Special Education Schools in Florida - Private schools for adhd in Coral Springs ](https://cdn.shortpixel.ai/spai/q_lossy+w_1200+to_webp+ret_img/centeracademy.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/Divider-Banner.png "Accredited special education schools in Florida - school for kids with adhd")

Center Academy began in 1968 with the vision of its founder,  
[Dr. Mack Hicks](https://centeracademy.com/team/mack-hicks/), who saw the need for a specialized,  
full-day school for students who were falling through the cracks in traditional schools.

![Center Academy Campus](https://cdn.shortpixel.ai/spai/q_lossy+w_810+to_webp+ret_img/centeracademy.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/CA-Coral-Springs_-61-copy.jpg "CA Coral Springs and Lutz")

### Independent Special Education School

**—**  
Center Academy is a private school offering specialized classes for middle-and high-school students with learning differences. Our small classes allow our teachers to accommodate each student’s unique learning style.

### Our Teachers Understand and Care

**—**  
At Center Academy, we begin each student’s education with an assessment of their learning style, their personality, and their previous experiences. Our teachers understand the nuances of a wide variety of learning differences such as ADHD, [Dyslexia and high-functioning Autism Spectrum Disorder/Asperger’s](https://autismguide.co.uk/latest/how-autism-aspergers-and-adhd-are-linked-with-dyslexia/) and work with each student to develop goals that are in line with their unique potential. We provide a caring, comfortable atmosphere, where each student is able to work toward their goals at their own pace, either in a small group setting, or with individualized instruction.

### Arrange for a Personal Tour of Center Academy

**—**  
A personal tour of Center Academy will show you why students love it here. We’d love to show you around our campus and introduce you to our staff. We can also review your child’s particular needs, discuss our admissions process, and see whether your child might qualify for one of many scholarship programs. Please call your nearest campus today to arrange a tour!

### Contact Us For More Information

text:
Center Academy in Florida - Mission Statement
Mission Statement
The mission of Center Academy is to provide students with a learning environment that facilitates the development of self-confidence, motivation, and academic skills, and gives students the opportunity to achieve success in life. To accomplish this mission, Center Academy is guided by the following principles and goals:
Center Academy will improve its students’ self-esteem, self-concept, and self-confidence.
Center Academy will improve student motivation levels and inspire students to give their best efforts in all tasks they undertake.
Center Academy will provide a “third place” for the student. A place beyond the home and school. It will provide a community of support for the young person. The community relationship will promote student, parent, and staff cohesiveness.
Center Academy will prepare students for post-graduate studies while emphasizing the essential skills of reading, writing, and mathematics.
Center Academy will remediate specific learning disabilities and other neuropsychological weaknesses, and at the same time teach compensatory strategies so that students can be successful as adults.
Center Academy will develop staff loyalty by ensuring that all staff members, part-time and full-time, understand the significance of their work and share the goals and values of the school.
Contact Us For More Information

# Center Academy in Florida - Mission Statement

![mission statement](https://cdn.shortpixel.ai/spai/q_lossy+w_80+to_webp+ret_img/centeracademy.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/05/CA-favicon.png "CA favicon")

## Mission Statement

![Special Education Schools in Florida - Private schools for adhd in Coral Springs ](https://cdn.shortpixel.ai/spai/q_lossy+w_1200+to_webp+ret_img/centeracademy.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/Divider-Banner.png "Accredited special education schools in Florida - school for kids with adhd")

The mission of Center Academy is to provide students with a learning environment that facilitates the development of self-confidence, motivation, and academic skills, and gives students the opportunity to achieve success in life. To accomplish this mission, Center Academy is guided by the following principles and goals:

Center Academy will improve its students’ self-esteem, self-concept, and self-confidence.

Center Academy will improve student motivation levels and inspire students to give their best efforts in all tasks they undertake.

Center Academy will provide a “third place” for the student. A place beyond the home and school. It will provide a community of support for the young person. The community relationship will promote student, parent, and staff cohesiveness.

Center Academy will prepare students for post-graduate studies while emphasizing the essential skills of reading, writing, and mathematics.

Center Academy will remediate specific learning disabilities and other neuropsychological weaknesses, and at the same time teach compensatory strategies so that students can be successful as adults.

Center Academy will develop staff loyalty by ensuring that all staff members, part-time and full-time, understand the significance of their work and share the goals and values of the school.

### Contact Us For More Information

Center Academy: Schools for Autism in Florida

text:
History of Center Academy
The History of Center Academy
1968

1968
Center Academy began in 1968 with the vision of its founder, Dr. Mack Hicks. Seeing the need for a specialized, full-day school for students who were falling through the cracks in traditional schools, Dr. Hicks established one of the first schools in the United States to focus on the needs of students with Attention Deficit Disorder and other neuropsychological processing difficulties.
With a team of professionals in the fields of neuropsychology, education, and individualized remediation, Center Academy opened its first classrooms in a small church and then expanded to the Science Center in St. Petersburg, Florida. The school focused on remediating learning disabilities and providing a small, focused, and positive learning environment for students who had experienced academic frustration, and for whom tutoring and other part-time programs were not successful.
1974

1974
Center Academy opened a campus in London, England in response to requests by parents and professionals. In the US, the St. Petersburg Center Academy moved to a larger site as the school expanded to meet the needs of more students. It moved to its permanent location in Pinellas Park, FL in 1974, finding a peaceful, 10-acre setting with horses and room to grow. In 1976, Center Academy established a campus in Tampa, FL.
1984

1984
Initially, Center Academy worked with elementary students who were struggling with large schools, ineffective teaching methods, or one-size-fits-all approaches to learning. Over the years, the scope of Center Academy expanded to include other students, and in 1984, Center Academy began its high school program.
1987

1987
The Pinellas Park and Tampa schools celebrated their first graduating class in 1987.
1988

1988
Center Academy was awarded a grant from the National Institute of Mental Health (NIMH) to develop and research the effectiveness of individualized remedial programs for students with a wide range of learning disabilities. While developing these programs, Center Academy provided free evaluations and remediation to over 200 students under the NIMH grant.
2023

2023
As Center Academy has expanded to locations around Florida, it has stayed true to its mission and kept its focus on addressing the unique needs of each student. Center Academy now has 11 schools in Florida. Hunters Creek in Orlando opened in August of 2021. Our newest campus in Cape Coral is accepting students now for the 23-24 school year.

# History of Center Academy

## The History of Center Academy

![Special Education Schools in Florida - Private schools for adhd in Coral Springs ](https://cdn.shortpixel.ai/spai/q_lossy+w_1200+to_webp+ret_img/centeracademy.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/Divider-Banner.png "Accredited special education schools in Florida - school for kids with adhd")

1968



1968

Center Academy began in 1968 with the vision of its founder, Dr. Mack Hicks. Seeing the need for a specialized, full-day school for students who were falling through the cracks in traditional schools, Dr. Hicks established one of the first schools in the United States to focus on the needs of students with Attention Deficit Disorder and other neuropsychological processing difficulties.

With a team of professionals in the fields of neuropsychology, education, and individualized remediation, Center Academy opened its first classrooms in a small church and then expanded to the Science Center in St. Petersburg, Florida. The school focused on remediating learning disabilities and providing a small, focused, and positive learning environment for students who had experienced academic frustration, and for whom tutoring and other part-time programs were not successful.

1974



1974

Center Academy opened a campus in London, England in response to requests by parents and professionals. In the US, the St. Petersburg Center Academy moved to a larger site as the school expanded to meet the needs of more students. It moved to its permanent location in Pinellas Park, FL in 1974, finding a peaceful, 10-acre setting with horses and room to grow. In 1976, Center Academy established a campus in Tampa, FL.

1984



1984

Initially, Center Academy worked with elementary students who were struggling with large schools, ineffective teaching methods, or one-size-fits-all approaches to learning. Over the years, the scope of Center Academy expanded to include other students, and in 1984, Center Academy began its high school program.

1987



1987

The Pinellas Park and Tampa schools celebrated their first graduating class in 1987.

1988



1988

Center Academy was awarded a grant from the [National Institute of Mental Health (NIMH)](https://www.nimh.nih.gov/) to develop and research the effectiveness of individualized remedial programs for students with a wide range of learning disabilities. While developing these programs, Center Academy provided free evaluations and remediation to over 200 students under the NIMH grant.

2023



2023

As Center Academy has expanded to locations around Florida, it has stayed true to its mission and kept its focus on addressing the unique needs of each student. Center Academy now has 11 schools in Florida. Hunters Creek in Orlando opened in August of 2021. Our newest campus in Cape Coral is accepting students now for the 23-24 school year.

Note from our Teachers: For us, that’s a goal because we know that every child is capable of succeeding, and we believe that all children learn differently.
Center Academy: Schools for Autism in Florida

text:
A Note From Our Teachers
We’re parents too! And as parents we know that when your child is hurting, you’re hurting. Whether your child’s frustration stems from negative school experiences or untapped potential, it hurts. We also know you have a dream for your child that includes happiness and a successful future. Sometimes our dreams get off track, but with love and support in a safe environment, your child can re-establish his or her learning ability and underlying goodness.
As your child’s teachers and mentors, we pledge to give you 100% of our effort and expertise. For us, that’s a reachable goal because we know that every child is capable of succeeding, and we believe that all children learn differently. We enjoy teaching children and adolescents. Our primary goal is helping your child grow and mature into a self-reliant adult.
If you want to speak to professionals who understand where you are coming from, visit our Center Academy classrooms and talk to us.
Keep that dream alive!
Your Center Academy Teachers
In addition to specialized training in working with students with learning differences, all Center Academy teachers have a Bachelor’s Degree or higher. While state education statute also allows instructors who have three or more years of public and/or private school teaching or have special skills, knowledge or expertise that qualifies them to provide instruction in subjects taught, Center Academy requires a minimum of a Bachelor’s Degree for all teachers.

# A Note From Our Teachers

We’re parents too! And as parents we know that when your child is hurting, you’re hurting. Whether your child’s frustration stems from negative school experiences or untapped potential, it hurts. We also know you have a dream for your child that includes happiness and a successful future. Sometimes our dreams get off track, but with love and support in a safe environment, your child can re-establish his or her learning ability and underlying goodness.

As your child’s teachers and mentors, we pledge to give you 100% of our effort and expertise. For us, that’s a reachable goal because we know that every child is capable of succeeding, and we believe that all children learn differently. We enjoy teaching children and adolescents. Our primary goal is helping your child grow and mature into a self-reliant adult.

If you want to speak to professionals who understand where you are coming from, visit our Center Academy classrooms and talk to us.

Keep that dream alive!

**Your Center Academy Teachers**

In addition to specialized training in working with students with learning differences, all Center Academy teachers have a Bachelor’s Degree or higher. While state education statute also allows instructors who have three or more years of public and/or private school teaching or have special skills, knowledge or expertise that qualifies them to provide instruction in subjects taught, Center Academy requires a minimum of a Bachelor’s Degree for all teachers.

metadata.title: Ethical Training and Reporting
metadata.description: Center Academy performs criminal-background checks on its employees and volunteers with unsupervised access to children.

openGraph.content: Center Academy performs criminal-background checks on its employees and volunteers with unsupervised access to children.
Center Academy: Schools for Autism in Florida

text:
Ethical Training and Reporting
Ethical Misconduct
Reporting of Ethical Misconduct is required by law and is our duty as
professionals entrusted with the safety and welfare of students every day.
Florida Statute section 39.203 and section 768.095 protect the person reporting ethical misconduct from reprisal by the individual reported or the employer if the report was made in good faith without malicious intent. Center Academy requires that every employee report any issue that could currently or potentially impact the health, safety, or welfare of its students and Center Academy employees.

The process for reporting is as follows. Any incident that an employee believes is potentially harmful to a student or another employee must be immediately reported to their immediate supervisor, the Regional Director, or in the absence of those persons, to the Corporate Office and President of Center Academy. Any employee who knowingly fails to report misconduct or abuse will be subject to discipline, up to and including termination. To report Employee or Administrator misconduct, contact: Mr. Steve Hicks: 813.695.1949. To report Administrator misconduct, contact Dr. Andrew Hicks or Dr. Eric Larson: 727.541.5716.

Parents, students, and employees should regularly visit the public registry to review it for individuals who may have prior criminal records and sex offenses. Information concerning registered sex offenders and predators in Florida may be obtained by visiting https://www.fdle.state.fl.us, the Florida Department of Law Enforcement Sexual Offenders database. Information may also be obtained by contacting the FDLE’s toll free telephone number: 1-888-FL-PREDATOR (1-888-357-7332).

The school performs criminal-background checks on its employees and volunteers with unsupervised access to children, but cannot attest to the background of the various parents with whom their child may associate away from school.

In cases of suspected physical abuse, sexual abuse or neglect the employee should report those issues immediately to the 1-800-96 Abuse hotline.

All employees will be required to complete ethics training on an annual basis as a condition of their employment with LLL Licensing Inc.
Contact Us For More Information

metadata.title: Center Academy's Discrimination Compliance Policy
metadata.description: Center Academy does not discriminate. Read Center Academy's Discrimination Compliance Policy here.

openGraph.content: Center Academy does not discriminate. Read Center Academy's Discrimination Compliance Policy here.
Center Academy: Schools for Autism in Florida

text:
Center Academy's Discrimination Compliance Policy
Center Academy
Does Not Discriminate
Center Academy does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, sex, religion, national or ethnic origin, age, disability, veteran’s status, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, or other protected classification under federal, state, or local law in the administration of its educational policies, admissions policies, scholarship programs, other school-administered programs, or employment conditions or practices.
School Compliance Officer
—
Questions regarding the school’s compliance with the application and administration of the school’s nondiscrimination policies should be directed to Steve Hicks, 6710 86th Avenue N., Pinellas Park, FL 33782, 813.695.1949, stevehicks@centeracademy.com. The Compliance Officer shall be responsible for coordinating the School’s effort to meet its responsibilities under this policy. The Compliance Officer will receive and investigate any complaints filed under this policy and make recommendations to the Director for the prompt and equitable resolution of complaints. The School will provide its Compliance Officer and staff with appropriate training on this policy and their obligations hereunder. Retaliation against an individual for filing a complaint under this policy or participating in the informal or formal resolution is prohibited.

A person (student, parent, employee, or applicant) may report a concern or allegation that he or she is being denied an equal opportunity to access the School’s education programs, activities or services, including employment opportunities.

A complaint may be reported to the Compliance Officer, an Administrator, or to a staff member. Any complaint received by an Administrator or staff member shall be promptly reported to the Compliance Officer for handling.

A complaint may be made verbally or in writing.

Timelines are flexible, but a person with a complaint should try to report it within thirty (30) calendar days after an alleged discriminatory incident.

While the School will try to handle the complaint as expeditiously as possible, there can be unexpected or unforeseen circumstances that cause delay.

This policy provides an individual the option of filing an informal complaint (verbally or in writing), which allows a fuller range of options in facilitating prompt resolution of an issue(s). It is the choice of the individual complaining whether to initially pursue an informal complaint procedure. The Compliance Officer, or his/her designee, shall explore an informal resolution with the complainant, which may involve providing advice to the complainant on how to handle the issue(s) or reviewing this policy with persons involved in the issue. If the matter involves the conduct of another person, an informal resolution could involve a meeting with the complainant and that person provided both individuals agreed. Resolution through this informal procedure will be promptly explored. Atany time, the complainant may request to end the informal procedure to move to the formal complaint process.

An individual may file a formal complaint (verbally or in writing) under this policy. The complainant should provide the following information: a statement of the complaint that identifies the issue, provides pertinent facts, identifies those involved, and states what remedy is sought. The Compliance Officer, or his/her designee (“Investigator”) shall begin a formal investigation into a complaint within three (3) business days of receipt. The Investigator shall interview the complainant and any respondent as well as witnesses reasonably believed to have relevant information. The Investigator shall also consider relevant documents submitted by any person interviewed. The respondent may submit a written response to the complaint within seven (7) calendar days of being notified of the complaint. At the conclusion of the investigation, the Compliance Officer shall submit the report of the investigator to the School Director along with recommendations as to whether and how this policy was violated and a proposed remedy for the situation. The School Director (or his/her designee) shall review the Compliance Officer’s report and issue a final decision, or, remand the matter back to the Investigator for further investigation. If remanded, the specific areas or issues to be investigated further shall be identified. Once the investigation is complete to the satisfaction of the School Director, he or she shall issue a final decision as to whether and how this policy was violated as well as any remedial corrective action that will be taken to redress the matter and prevent further violations. Any corrective action must be age-appropriate and timely. The decision of the School Director shall be final and shall be communicated to the complainant and respondent separately as directed by the School Director.
