text:
Parent Resources | Foundation Academy
Skip to content
Quick Links Resources
Click the plus to expand the view to see the resources available for each heading. PDF lists and forms can be downloaded, printed, and returned to the appropriate departments or persons. 
In the event you are in need of additional help please feel free to visit our Campuses page and contact the specific campus regarding the answers to the questions you have.
School Year Academic Calendar
Download 2025-26 Academic Calendar
Download 2026-27 Academic Calendar
Elementary Summer Reading & Math
Were you aware that many students experience a decline in reading skills of a month or more during the summer break? Fortunately, by keeping children engaged with books, we can prevent this decline and ensure their academic growth continues.
This summer, we’re implementing some reading requirements for all students from Kindergarten(K5) to Sixth grade to continue growth in reading. Each grade level will have specific age-appropriate reading goals. We encourage you to collaborate with your child to make this summer reading experience enjoyable. Summer reading assignments will be given to the classroom teacher during the first week of school. 
Please note that AR quizzes cannot be completed over the summer break. However, students can take these quizzes upon their return in the first week of school. While these points won’t count towards their first-quarter goal, they will contribute to the total for the 2025-2026 school year.
Summer Reading and Math
Tilden Campus 6th Grade Summer Reading
Download the 6th Grade Summer Reading Requirements
Senior Resources
Senior Resources
Lower School Field Trips
Plant Street Field Trips
Lakeside Field Trips
School Supply Lists
Plant Street Campus
Our Plant Street campus has partnered with Impacks for school supplies. Skip the trip to the store and have all of your school supplies ready for pickup at the open house.
Foundation Academy Pre-School 2025-2026 Supply List
Foundation Academy Plant Street Supply List 2025-2026 (K5-5th Grade)
Lakeside Campus
Foundation Academy Lakeside Supply List 2025-2026 (K5-5th Grade)
Tilden Campus
Foundation Academy Tilden Campus students will receive specific supply lists at Meet the Teacher and More.
School Uniforms
View the Uniform Guidelines
After School Care
After School Care Information and Sign-up
Attendance
If your child is absent from school in grades 6-12 and attends our Tilden Campus, email attendance@foundationacademy.net. Indicate your student’s name, grade level, date of absence, and the reason for the absence.
If your child is absent from school in grades PK3 – 5th and attends our Plant Street Campus, send a written excuse to the homeroom teacher and send a written excuse to Nurse Megan at megan.engelbert@foundationacademy.net AND plantoffice@foundationacademy.net. Indicate your student’s name, grade level, date of absence, and the reason for the absence.
If your child is absent from school in grades K – 5th and attends our Lakeside Campus, send a written excuse to the homeroom teacher and send a written excuse to Nurse Ana Cordeiro anabarbara.cordeiro@foundationacademy.net. Indicate your student’s name, grade level, date of absence, and the reason for the absence.
A valid excuse is due within 24 hours of the student’s return to school.
Pre-Arranged Absence Forms
Lower School Pre-Arranged Absence Form
Upper School Pre-Arranged Absence Form
If your child is absent from school in grades 6-12, email attendance@foundationacademy.net. Indicate your student’s name, grade level, date of absence, and the reason for the absence.
If your child is absent from school in grades PK3 – 5th, send a written excuse to the homeroom teacher or send a written excuse to your campus administrator. Indicate your student’s name, grade level, date of absence, and the reason for the absence.
A valid excuse is due within 24 hours of the student’s return to school.

markdown:
# Parent Resources | Foundation Academy

[Skip to content](https://www.foundationacademy.net/parent-resources/#primary)

![Love Logic Header](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Love-Logic-Header.jpg)

## Quick Links Resources

Click the plus to expand the view to see the resources available for each heading. PDF lists and forms can be downloaded, printed, and returned to the appropriate departments or persons.

In the event you are in need of additional help please feel free to visit our [Campuses](https://www.foundationacademy.net/about/campuses/) page and contact the specific campus regarding the answers to the questions you have.

### School Year Academic Calendar

[Download 2025-26 Academic Calendar](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/2025-2026-Academic-Calendar.pdf)  
[Download 2026-27 Academic Calendar](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/2627-Parent-Calendar.pdf)

### Elementary Summer Reading & Math

Were you aware that many students experience a decline in reading skills of a month or more during the summer break? Fortunately, by keeping children engaged with books, we can prevent this decline and ensure their academic growth continues.

This summer, we’re implementing some reading requirements for all students from Kindergarten(K5) to Sixth grade to continue growth in reading. Each grade level will have specific age-appropriate reading goals. We encourage you to collaborate with your child to make this summer reading experience enjoyable. Summer reading assignments will be given to the classroom teacher during the first week of school. 

Please note that AR quizzes cannot be completed over the summer break. However, students can take these quizzes upon their return in the first week of school. While these points won’t count towards their first-quarter goal, they will contribute to the total for the 2025-2026 school year.

[Summer Reading and Math](https://www.foundationacademy.net/summer-reading/)

### Tilden Campus 6th Grade Summer Reading

[Download the 6th Grade Summer Reading Requirements](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Summer-Reading_6_Tilden.pdf)

### Senior Resources

[Senior Resources](https://www.foundationacademy.net/seniors/)

### Lower School Field Trips

[Plant Street Field Trips](https://www.foundationacademy.net/plant-street-field-trips/)  
[Lakeside Field Trips](https://www.foundationacademy.net/lakeside-field-trips)

### School Supply Lists

#### Plant Street Campus

Our Plant Street campus has partnered with Impacks for school supplies. Skip the trip to the store and have all of your school supplies ready for pickup at the open house.

[Foundation Academy Pre-School 2025-2026 Supply List](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Plant-Street-Preschool-Supply-List-25-26.pdf)

[Foundation Academy Plant Street Supply List 2025-2026 (_K5-5th Grade_)](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Plant-Street-K-5-Supply-List-25-26.pdf)

#### Lakeside Campus

[Foundation Academy Lakeside Supply List 2025-2026 (_K5-5th Grade_)](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/FA-Lakeside-2025-2026-Supply-List.pdf)

#### Tilden Campus

Foundation Academy Tilden Campus students will receive specific supply lists at Meet the Teacher and More.

### School Uniforms

[View the Uniform Guidelines](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/24-25-Uniform-Sheet-Final.pdf)

### After School Care

[After School Care Information and Sign-up](https://www.foundationacademy.net/student-life/aftercare)

### Attendance

If your child is absent from school in **grades 6-12** and attends our **Tilden Campus**, email [attendance@foundationacademy.net](mailto:attendance@foundationacademy.net). Indicate your student’s name, grade level, date of absence, and the reason for the absence.

If your child is absent from school in grades **PK3 – 5th** and attends our **Plant Street Campus**, send a written excuse to the homeroom teacher and send a written excuse to Nurse Megan at [](mailto:megan.englebert@foundationacademy.net)[megan.engelbert@foundationacademy.net](mailto:megan.engelbert@foundationacademy.net) AND [plantoffice@foundationacademy.net](mailto:plantoffice@foundationacademy.net). Indicate your student’s name, grade level, date of absence, and the reason for the absence.

If your child is absent from school in grades **K – 5th** and attends our **Lakeside Campus**, send a written excuse to the homeroom teacher and send a written excuse to Nurse Ana Cordeiro [anabarbara.cordeiro@foundationacademy.net](mailto:anabarbara.cordeiro@foundationacademy.net). Indicate your student’s name, grade level, date of absence, and the reason for the absence.

A valid excuse is due within 24 hours of the student’s return to school.

### Pre-Arranged Absence Forms

[Lower School Pre-Arranged Absence Form](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/09/Lower-School-Pre-Arranged-Absence-Form.pdf)

[Upper School Pre-Arranged Absence Form](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/23_24-PRE-ARRANGED-ABSENCE-REQUEST-FORM-3.pdf)

If your child is absent from school in **grades 6-12**, email [attendance@foundationacademy.net](mailto:attendance@foundationacademy.net). Indicate your student’s name, grade level, date of absence, and the reason for the absence.

If your child is absent from school in grades **PK3 – 5th**, send a written excuse to the homeroom teacher or send a written excuse to your campus administrator. Indicate your student’s name, grade level, date of absence, and the reason for the absence.

A valid excuse is due within 24 hours of the student’s return to school.

### FACTS-SIS Help

To make changes to Family or Student Information, [click here](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/Steps-for-Parents-to-Make-Changes-in-Family-or-Student-Info-in-RenWeb.pdf).

To log in to check their Payment Plans and/or Apply for Tuition Assistance, [click here](https://online.factsmgt.com/signin/3V6TC).

### Reporting of Abuse & Misconduct

[Abuse & Misconduct](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/2022-FA-Child-Abuse-Awareness-Web-Content.pdf)

### Employee Ethics Training & Code of Conduct

[Employee Ethics Training & Code of Conduct](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Ethics-and-Child-Abuse-2023.pdf)

### High School Student Parking Form

[High School Student Parking Form](https://www.foundationacademy.net/student-parking-form/)

### Room Parent Guide

[Room Parent Guide](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/Room-Parent-Training-5.pdf)

## Ready to **Tour** Our School?

Foundation Academy has three incredible campuses to explore—all centered around quality Christian education. Schedule a tour today to get started.

![FA Classroom scaled](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/FA-Classroom-scaled.jpg)

At Foundation Academy, we have a lot to share. Request more information today to get the full scope of what our school has to offer.

text:
Windermere Private School | Foundation Lakeside Campus
Skip to content
Our Newest FA Location
In conjunction with the partnership with Family Church of Windermere, our Lakeside Campus opened at the beginning of 2019 and is our newest location at Foundation Academy. Our Windermere private school is geared toward Kindergarten through 5th grade.
Our Lakeside Campus sits just off the 535 and is located at 8464 Winter Garden Vineland Rd, Orlando, FL 32836, sharing the property of Family Church Windermere. Of our three campuses, Lakeside is the most easily accessible for Disney employees — only about a 10-minute ride away. 
The Highlights
The Foundation Academy Lakeside campus is located in the vicinity of beautiful Windermere/Orlando, the campus is just the right size to broaden your child’s world just a little bit more as we challenge them to grow.
Imaginative learning stations that spark curiosity and help the students engage the learning process which promotes growth. 
An outdoor garden classroom that provides the students with valuable learning & lessons that mirror their own growth and reinforces God’s principles of reap and sow. 
Small class sizes that lead to more one on one attention that is paramount in the development of early learning. 
Hear It from Our Lakeside Students
“Our experience at Lakeside has been absolutely amazing! To have not just one but two wonderful educators devoted to our daughter and her four classmates is beyond anything we could have imagined. ”
The Kaminski Family 
Helpful Links
A Great Location at Every Campus
Need to get to more than one of our Foundation Academy campuses? All three of our locations benefit from easily accessible entrances without heavy congestion or major inconveniences and can all be reached from one another in about 10 minutes. For families with children attending multiple Foundation Academy campuses, our close proximity to each other makes dropoff/pickup highly efficient for everyone. 
Ready to Tour Our School?
Foundation Academy has three incredible campuses to explore—all centered around quality Christian education. Schedule a tour today to get started.
Want to Learn More About FA?
At Foundation Academy, we have a lot to share. Request more information today to get the full scope of what our school has to offer.
markdown:
# Windermere Private School | Foundation Lakeside Campus

[Skip to content](https://www.foundationacademy.net/about/campuses/lakeside-campus/#primary)

![Lakside Hdr](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/07/Lakside_Hdr.jpg)

## Our **Newest** FA Location

In conjunction with the partnership with Family Church of Windermere, our Lakeside Campus opened at the beginning of 2019 and is our newest location at Foundation Academy. Our Windermere private school is geared toward Kindergarten through 5th grade.

Our Lakeside Campus sits just off the 535 and is located at [8464 Winter Garden Vineland Rd, Orlando, FL 32836](https://www.google.com/maps/place/Foundation+Academy+-+Lakeside+Campus/@28.4409946,-81.5516127,15z/data=!4m2!3m1!1s0x0:0x47de2129a1d9b414?sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwjrjpPEvc7tAhUySN8KHUvcC5gQ_BIwCnoECBUQBQ), sharing the property of Family Church Windermere. Of our three campuses, Lakeside is the most easily accessible for Disney employees — only about a 10-minute ride away. 

![Lakeside Highlight Graphic](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/07/Lakeside_Highlight_Graphic.jpg)

## The **Highlights**

The Foundation Academy Lakeside campus is located in the vicinity of beautiful Windermere/Orlando, the campus is just the right size to broaden your child’s world just a little bit more as we challenge them to grow.

*   **Imaginative learning stations** that spark curiosity and help the students engage the learning process which promotes growth.
*   An **outdoor garden classroom** that provides the students with valuable learning & lessons that mirror their own growth and reinforces God’s principles of reap and sow.
*   **Small class sizes** that lead to more one on one attention that is paramount in the development of early learning.

![Lakeside Testimony 2](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/07/Lakeside-Testimony-2.jpg)

#### Hear It from Our Lakeside Students

> “Our experience at Lakeside has been absolutely amazing!  To have not just one but two wonderful educators devoted to our daughter and her four classmates is beyond anything we could have imagined. ”
> 
> The Kaminski Family 

## Helpful **Links**

## A **Great Location** at Every Campus

Need to get to more than one of our Foundation Academy campuses? All three of our locations benefit from easily accessible entrances without heavy congestion or major inconveniences and can all be reached from one another in about 10 minutes. For families with children attending multiple Foundation Academy campuses, our close proximity to each other makes dropoff/pickup highly efficient for everyone. 

![380 MG 0334](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/380__MG_0334.jpg)

## Ready to **Tour** Our School?

Foundation Academy has three incredible campuses to explore—all centered around quality Christian education. Schedule a tour today to get started.

![225 MG 0245](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/225__MG_0245.jpg)

## Want to **Learn** More About FA?

At Foundation Academy, we have a lot to share. Request more information today to get the full scope of what our school has to offer.

metadata.title: Foundation Academy Fine Arts Program | Christian Private School
metadata.description: The Foundation Academy Fine Arts Program offers many ways for students to get involved in the arts. We offer a variety of courses from theatre, band to robotics and radio journalism.

openGraph.content: Foundation Academy Fine Arts Program | Christian Private School
The Foundation Academy Fine Arts Program offers many ways for students to get involved in the arts. We offer a variety of courses from theatre, band to robotics and radio journalism.
Children finger painting using easels in a classroom

text:
Foundation Academy Fine Arts Program
Celebrating Creativity
Throughout all of history, the vision of invention and progress was a result of creative thought and action. Nowhere is this more evident than in the first chapter of Genesis where God, the Master Creator of all things, spoke Heaven and Earth into being.
The Foundation Academy Fine Arts Department celebrates the excellence modeled for us in God’s creation. Each student is encouraged to stretch his or her imagination and expand his or her own ingenuity. While art celebrates the individuality of each student, a prudent curriculum is both aesthetically and practically educational.
Student Involvement
At Foundation Academy, the Fine Arts Department involves the students on three levels:
A cognitive level in which students gain practical art skills
An intuitive level where each student is allowed to express his or her own individuality
A spiritual level that draws them to a closer walk with God as they tap into His creative nature
Foundation Academy offers a wide variety of classes in the Arts for grades PK – 12 in performing, visual, and multi media arts.
Our Fine Arts Opportunities
Choir
Through our choir programs, students not only get a comprehensive understanding of music and proper technique, but they also learn the valuable role music plays in a worship service.
Digital Arts
We know fine arts expand into the digital arena, too. That’s why we offer opportunities to learn Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, and more to expand on their digital creativity early on.
Multimedia & TV
Lights, camera, action! Foundation Academy also offers immersive ways for students to get involved in pre-and post-production, premiere, and audio-visual coursework.
Theatre Arts
From The Glass Menagerie to Disney’s The Little Mermaid and beyond, students between the 2nd and 12th grades can delight in the art of storytelling through theatre.
Visual Arts
Our visual arts students are given the freedom to express themselves through a variety of mediums in 2D and 3D, exploring painting, drawing, clay, mixed media, and more.
Steel Drums
Foundation Steel has become one of the most popular programs in the arts. Mr. James McKey leads the program which utilizes the unique sounds of the steel drums to learn and cover a diverse range of music!
Praise Band
Praise Band for upper school students is designed to teach students the meaning of worship and the role music plays in a worship service. Praise Band teaches each student about their leadership responsibilities on and off the platform.
Want to Join the Fine Arts League?
Our goal is to foster passion in our students who embrace the arts. Fine Arts League membership provides our Fine Arts Department with additional financial resources to develop our students further, teachers, and the resources necessary to reach their full creativity.
Ready to Tour Our School?
Foundation Academy has three incredible campuses to explore—all centered around quality Christian education. Schedule a tour today to get started.
Want to Learn More About FA?
At Foundation Academy, we have a lot to share. Request more information today to get the full scope of what our school has to offer.

Throughout all of history, the vision of invention and progress was a result of creative thought and action. Nowhere is this more evident than in the first chapter of Genesis where God, the Master Creator of all things, spoke Heaven and Earth into being.

](https://www.foundationacademy.net/arts/theatre-arts)[

](https://www.foundationacademy.net/arts/visual-arts)[

logo.caption: Foundation Academy

@graph.name: Foundation Academy | Private Christian School in Winter Garden

text:
Foundation Academy | Private Christian School in Winter Garden
Elementary School

Foundation Academy’s Elementary School, a leading Private Christian School in Winter Garden, offers a nurturing, Christ-centered education for students in grades K–5 across two beautiful campuses: Plant Street and Lakeside. At Foundation Academy, we are committed to developing each child’s mind, heart, and spirit through rigorous academics, biblical teaching, and a loving community. Whether your child is just beginning their educational journey or continuing to grow in faith and knowledge, our elementary programs provide a safe and supportive environment where students thrive both spiritually and academically.
Academics & Honors Program
All curriculum is carefully chosen for each subject with the highest academic goals in mind. A variety of publishers, both Christian and secular, are used. All subjects are taught from a Biblical worldview. Educational projects and field trips are offered at each grade level that enhances curriculum content.
An Honors Program is available to eligible students in 3rd through 5th grade, while 1st and 2nd grade students may participate in the Mini-Honors Program. Admission into these programs is based on a combination of standardized test scores, reading levels, and teacher recommendations.
Designed to challenge and inspire, the Honors Program incorporates integrative technology and advanced critical-thinking tools that encourage students to go beyond traditional learning. These programs provide an elevated academic experience, preparing students to excel both inside and outside the classroom. Participation requires an additional tuition fee.
Enrichment Classes & More
Elementary students also benefit from a rich variety of enrichment classes that complement their core academics and nurture the whole child. Through art, they explore creativity and self-expression; in music, they develop an appreciation for rhythm, performance, and collaboration; during physical education, they build healthy habits, teamwork, and confidence; and in STEM, they engage in hands-on problem solving that sparks curiosity and innovation. Together, these experiences provide a well-rounded foundation that helps students discover their gifts and passions.
Chapel & Worship
Chapel is held weekly at both the Plant St. and Lakeside campuses. It is an exciting time of praise and worship is followed by an engaging message centered on thematic lessons from the Bible with practical life application. Guest speakers include pastors from local churches as well as members of the FA Faculty who pour into the lives of the students each week. Parents and Students are encouraged to worship together during this special time. 
Life at Foundation Academy
At Foundation Academy we have methodically built a culture that takes into consideration growth and development from childhood to young adulthood. Our goal is to provide a broad, dynamic range of experiences that are a testament to who they are as individuals and how their gifts, talents, and passions fit into God’s perfect will for their lives.
Life at our Plant Street Campus lies in the beautiful setting of the historic downtown Winter Garden. The tree-lined brick streets, mom & pop stores, and intimacy of the community make it the ideal setting for early childhood development.
Life at our Lakeside Campus is rooted in the vibrant community of Windermere, nestled within an area of exciting growth and new development. Lakeside offers a safe and nurturing environment where students can learn, explore, and thrive. The combination of a close-knit community and a forward-looking setting makes it the perfect place for young learners to begin their educational journey.
Other Elementary Highlights
Foundation Academy has a deep belief in students having the opportunity to develop their passions and skills after school. Our primary objective is to utilize their God-given interests and abilities and embrace them by showing them the attention and focus they deserve. We have placed qualified driven instructors and coaches to guide them in their daily growth.
Lions Pride Development Athletic Program 
Our Elementary Sports Program is a concentration of team sports such as Basketball, Soccer, Volleyball, and Flag Football. The program is designed with the intent to teach the fundamentals of each sport and help each young athlete identify where their passions and skills lie that they may begin to choose a path that suits both. The sports are seasonal and are complimented with both workouts and games. 
The program is headed up by Brandon Venetta.
After School Clubs
After-school clubs at Foundation Academy provide students with opportunities to pursue their passions, build new skills, and connect with friends outside the classroom. From the National Elementary Honor Society and Chess Club to creative outlets like Drama, Art, and Graphic Design, and hands-on exploration through Lego Club, there is something for every interest. These programs enrich the student experience by encouraging leadership, collaboration, and creativity.
Plant Street Campus Clubs
Lakeside Campus Clubs
Field Trips
Field trips at Foundation Academy extend learning beyond the classroom, giving students opportunities to explore, discover, and connect classroom concepts to the world around them. Each trip is designed to enrich their education and spark curiosity. Families can view upcoming trips and complete registration online.
Plant Street Campus Field Trips
Lakeside Campus Field Trips
Hear It from Our Elementary school students
“What I love most about my school is I am able to be myself every day and feel like I am a part of something that feels like family.”
Addison White, 6th Grade
Helpful Links
Ready to Tour Our School?
Foundation Academy has three incredible campuses to explore—all centered around quality Christian education. Schedule a tour today to get started.
Want to Learn More About FA?
At Foundation Academy, we have a lot to share. Request more information today to get the full scope of what our school has to offer.
markdown:
# Foundation Academy | Private Christian School in Winter Garden

![Kids in classroom using computers](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/Kids-in-classroom-using-computers.jpg)

## Elementary School

  
Foundation Academy’s Elementary School, a leading Private Christian School in Winter Garden, offers a nurturing, Christ-centered education for students in grades K–5 across two beautiful campuses: Plant Street and Lakeside. At Foundation Academy, we are committed to developing each child’s mind, heart, and spirit through rigorous academics, biblical teaching, and a loving community. Whether your child is just beginning their educational journey or continuing to grow in faith and knowledge, our elementary programs provide a safe and supportive environment where students thrive both spiritually and academically.

## Academics & **Honors** Program

All curriculum is carefully chosen for each subject with the highest academic goals in mind. A variety of publishers, both Christian and secular, are used. All subjects are taught from a Biblical worldview. Educational projects and field trips are offered at each grade level that enhances curriculum content.

An Honors Program is available to eligible students in 3rd through 5th grade, while 1st and 2nd grade students may participate in the Mini-Honors Program. Admission into these programs is based on a combination of standardized test scores, reading levels, and teacher recommendations.

Designed to challenge and inspire, the Honors Program incorporates integrative technology and advanced critical-thinking tools that encourage students to go beyond traditional learning. These programs provide an elevated academic experience, preparing students to excel both inside and outside the classroom. Participation requires an additional tuition fee.

![Private Christian School Winter Garden](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/Teacher-and-kid-smiling-1024x929.jpg)

![Kids Playing Instrument](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/Kids-Playing-Instrument-1024x769.jpg)

## ****Enrichment**** Classes & More

Elementary students also benefit from a rich variety of enrichment classes that complement their core academics and nurture the whole child. Through art, they explore creativity and self-expression; in music, they develop an appreciation for rhythm, performance, and collaboration; during physical education, they build healthy habits, teamwork, and confidence; and in STEM, they engage in hands-on problem solving that sparks curiosity and innovation. Together, these experiences provide a well-rounded foundation that helps students discover their gifts and passions.

## Chapel & ****Worship****

Chapel is held weekly at both the Plant St. and Lakeside campuses. It is an exciting time of praise and worship is followed by an engaging message centered on thematic lessons from the Bible with practical life application. Guest speakers include pastors from local churches as well as members of the FA Faculty who pour into the lives of the students each week. Parents and Students are encouraged to worship together during this special time. 

![Chapel Worship](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/Chapel-Worship-1024x767.jpg)

![About 1](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/About_1.jpg)

## ****Life**** at Foundation Academy

At Foundation Academy we have methodically built a culture that takes into consideration growth and development from childhood to young adulthood. Our goal is to provide a broad, dynamic range of experiences that are a testament to who they are as individuals and how their gifts, talents, and passions fit into God’s perfect will for their lives.

Life at our Plant Street Campus lies in the beautiful setting of the historic downtown Winter Garden. The tree-lined brick streets, mom & pop stores, and intimacy of the community make it the ideal setting for early childhood development.

Life at our Lakeside Campus is rooted in the vibrant community of Windermere, nestled within an area of exciting growth and new development. Lakeside offers a safe and nurturing environment where students can learn, explore, and thrive. The combination of a close-knit community and a forward-looking setting makes it the perfect place for young learners to begin their educational journey.

## Other Elementary **Highlights**

Foundation Academy has a deep belief in students having the opportunity to develop their passions and skills after school. Our primary objective is to utilize their God-given interests and abilities and embrace them by showing them the attention and focus they deserve. We have placed qualified driven instructors and coaches to guide them in their daily growth.

### Lions Pride Development Athletic Program

Our Elementary Sports Program is a concentration of team sports such as Basketball, Soccer, Volleyball, and Flag Football. The program is designed with the intent to teach the fundamentals of each sport and help each young athlete identify where their passions and skills lie that they may begin to choose a path that suits both. The sports are seasonal and are complimented with both workouts and games.

The program is headed up by Brandon Venetta.

### After School Clubs

After-school clubs at Foundation Academy provide students with opportunities to pursue their passions, build new skills, and connect with friends outside the classroom. From the National Elementary Honor Society and Chess Club to creative outlets like Drama, Art, and Graphic Design, and hands-on exploration through Lego Club, there is something for every interest. These programs enrich the student experience by encouraging leadership, collaboration, and creativity.

[Plant Street Campus Clubs](https://www.foundationacademy.net/plant-street-campus-clubs)

[Lakeside Campus Clubs](https://www.foundationacademy.net/lakeside-campus-clubs)

### Field Trips

Field trips at Foundation Academy extend learning beyond the classroom, giving students opportunities to explore, discover, and connect classroom concepts to the world around them. Each trip is designed to enrich their education and spark curiosity. Families can view upcoming trips and complete registration online.

[Plant Street Campus Field Trips](https://www.foundationacademy.net/plant-street-field-trips)

[Lakeside Campus Field Trips](https://www.foundationacademy.net/lakeside-field-trips)

![Elementary Testimonial Pic](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/07/Elementary-Testimonial-Pic-1280x1083.jpg)

#### Hear It from Our Elementary school students

> “What I love most about my school is I am able to be myself every day and feel like I am a part of something that feels like family.”
> 
> Addison White, 6th Grade

text:
Box Office | Foundation Academy
Skip to content
Know Before You Go
Thank you for your interest in attending a Foundation Academy Production. Here are some helpful tips to making your event experience the best it can be.
Buy Your Tickets Early – Seating is limited and assigned. Many of our shows do sell out, especially in our VIP seating section. You will want to buy your tickets below ahead of time, as we often have to turn guests away at the door when seating is not available.
Arrive on Time – Doors close promptly at show time. If you arrive after the show has begun, you will not be able to find you seat and will need to stand or sit in the overflow section until intermission.
Please Stay Seated – For the safety of everyone, including our performers, please plan to stay seated until the intermission or the show has concluded. In order to create a more immersive experience, our actors enter the scene from the back of house. The theatre is also very dark, and leaving in the middle of a scene can be very distracting to those around you. If you do need to leave for any reason, please wait until the scene or song has concluded.

# Box Office | Foundation Academy

[Skip to content](https://www.foundationacademy.net/box-office/#primary)

![Theatre Curtains](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/TheatreCurtains.jpg)

## Know Before You Go

Thank you for your interest in attending a Foundation Academy Production. Here are some helpful tips to making your event experience the best it can be.

*   **Buy Your Tickets Early** – Seating is limited and assigned. Many of our shows do sell out, especially in our VIP seating section. You will want to buy your tickets below ahead of time, as we often have to turn guests away at the door when seating is not available.
*   **Arrive on Time** – Doors close promptly at show time. If you arrive after the show has begun, you will not be able to find you seat and will need to stand or sit in the overflow section until intermission.
*   **Please Stay Seated** – For the safety of everyone, including our performers, please plan to stay seated until the intermission or the show has concluded. In order to create a more immersive experience, our actors enter the scene from the back of house. The theatre is also very dark, and leaving in the middle of a scene can be very distracting to those around you. If you do need to leave for any reason, please wait until the scene or song has concluded.

## Upcoming Performances

text:
Choir | Foundation Academy
Skip to content
The Foundation Academy Upper School Choir Program is designed to come along students and help them explore choral music through study and performance. As we learn from King David in the Book of Psalms, the study of music and lifting song up to the Lord provides sensory, mental, emotional, and social experiences beneficial to any who take part. It is the goal of this program to provide a safe, Christian environment for students to use their talents to glorify God.
6th Grade Choir
Exciting NEW 6th Grade Choir Opportunity! This year, students may opt to do the “wheel” AND participate in choir. Choir students have been approved to be pulled from their elective twice a week to rehearse. Students will learn vocal technique, a variety of musical genres, sight singing, and more! 
Tilden Choir (7-12)
Our award-winning Tilden Campus Choir has performed all over the Central Florida area bringing their artistry and passion to the community. From National Prayer Day events to the Winter Garden Christmas celebration our choir has helped bring the joy of music and song to many people.
We have had members of our choir recognized at the all-state level as well as have our entire choir perform at the famed Carnegie Hall. For the holidays our students have performed on local television.
Our choir program is centered on taking individuals who are driven to improve their musical skills and put them with others in an environment thats fosters learning, growth and as sense of team.
Program Highlights
Create music independently and with others 
Improve vocal technique 
Become more musically proficient in terms of reading, interpreting, and expressing music of all different styles 
Perform on a consistent basis 
Learn qualities of leadership and integrity as related to being a part of a musical team
Constant and Consistent Growth
Middle & High School Choirs are designed to teach and improve students’ vocal and performance skills.
Ensemble and Individual Vocal Performance
Application to those skills is challenged daily in both ensemble and individual vocal performance. This is accomplished by teaching vocal technique, mechanics, and anatomy of the voice.
Aural Interval Recognition
Ear training is also an essential part of the student’s training. Aural interval recognition is integrated with visual recognition of music notation. It is our goal for all students to become musically literate.
Student Testimonials
“Throughout my four years in the choral program, I blossomed as a musician and a young adult. We sang and learned all types of genres including contemporary Christian, 50s music, 80s music, pop, and you cannot forget Christmas!! …”

# Choir | Foundation Academy

[Skip to content](https://www.foundationacademy.net/student-life/arts/choir/#primary)

![Choir 02 scaled](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/07/Choir-02-scaled.jpg)

The **Foundation Academy Upper School Choir Program** is designed to come along students and help them explore choral music through study and performance. As we learn from **King David** in the **Book of Psalms**, the study of music and lifting song up to the Lord provides sensory, mental, emotional, and social experiences beneficial to any who take part. It is the goal of this program to provide a safe, Christian environment for students to use their talents to glorify God.

## 6th Grade Choir

Exciting **NEW** 6th Grade Choir Opportunity! This year, students may opt to do the “wheel” AND participate in choir.  Choir students have been approved to be pulled from their elective twice a week to rehearse.  Students will learn vocal technique, a variety of musical genres, sight singing, and more! 

![Treblemakers Pic 2](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/07/Treblemakers-Pic-2-1024x994.jpg)

![Foundation Academy Tilden Choir](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/Foundation-Academy-Tilden-Choir-1024x683.jpg)

## Tilden Choir (7-12)

Our award-winning Tilden Campus Choir has performed all over the Central Florida area bringing their artistry and passion to the community. From National Prayer Day events to the Winter Garden Christmas celebration our choir has helped bring the joy of music and song to many people.

We have had members of our choir recognized at the all-state level as well as have our entire choir perform at the famed Carnegie Hall. For the holidays our students have performed on local television.

Our choir program is centered on taking individuals who are driven to improve their musical skills and put them with others in an environment thats fosters learning, growth and as sense of team.

## Program **Highlights**

*   **Create music** independently and with others 
*   Improve **vocal technique**   
*   Become more **musically proficient** in terms of reading, interpreting, and expressing music of all different styles 
*   **Perform** on a consistent basis  
*   Learn qualities of **leadership and integrity** as related to being a part of a musical team

### Constant and Consistent Growth

Middle & High School Choirs are designed to teach and improve students’ vocal and performance skills.

### Ensemble and Individual Vocal Performance

Application to those skills is challenged daily in both ensemble and individual vocal performance. This is accomplished by teaching vocal technique, mechanics, and anatomy of the voice.

### Aural Interval Recognition

Ear training is also an essential part of the student’s training. Aural interval recognition is integrated with visual recognition of music notation. It is our goal for all students to become musically literate.

![Choir Testimony Pic](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/07/Choir_Testimony_Pic-1280x1269.jpg)

#### Student Testimonials

> “Throughout my four years in the choral program, I blossomed as a musician and a young adult. We sang and learned all types of genres including contemporary Christian, 50s music, 80s music, pop, and you cannot forget Christmas!! …”
> 
> Savannah Doshan, Tilden Chior & Tri-M Member, Class of 2020

Explore more of the Arts that FA has to offer.

![student girl with easel painting at art school PVPQ9UA scaled](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/student-girl-with-easel-painting-at-art-school-PVPQ9UA-scaled.jpg)

metadata.title: Foundation Academy High School | Private Christian School
metadata.description: Tilden is Foundation Academy’s high school campus, offering a range of accelerated coursework options, as well as athletic and extracurricular opportunities.

openGraph.content: Foundation Academy High School | Private Christian School
Tilden is Foundation Academy’s high school campus, offering a range of accelerated coursework options, as well as athletic and extracurricular opportunities.

text:
Foundation Academy High School | Private Christian School
Academics
The upper school academic program at Foundation Academy is structured for students to earn a college-preparatory diploma in an engaging educational environment. The curriculum is designed to develop students who can reason, research, express, and critically think in order to prepare them for collegiate rigor. The faculty consists of committed Christians who are experienced and passionate about their subject area and teach with a Biblical worldview.
Foundation Academy offers a variety of options for accelerated coursework at the high school level. Students may participate in Honors classes, Advanced Placement (AP) courses, and Dual Enrollment (DE) courses through Southeastern University. Participation in AP and DE courses allow students the opportunity to begin earning their first college credits while still in high school. Foundation Academy also partners with Orange Technical College for students interested in pursuing trade-related careers. Elective offerings consist of the fine arts, foreign languages, and technology areas.
Extracurriculars
Foundation Academy is continuously adding to what we refer to as “FA After 3”, after school programs, and extracurricular activities. Each one meant to complement the Foundation Academy experience and intended to engage each student’s passions and interests. 
Upper School students participate in dozens of clubs and student organizations. Some clubs are chapters of national organizations or activities. Other clubs are just for student enjoyment or provide a service to the community or school.
Athletics
It is the desire for Foundation Academy athletics to offer athletes a program that provides a variety of sports with godly coaches who are experts in their field. Coaches that understand the importance of developing fierce competitors in the playing arena, as well as in the Kingdom of God.
At Foundation Academy, student-athletes are pushed to develop what is referred to as THE HEART OF A LION, a bold heart that confronts adversity with strength, dignity, and wisdom; desiring to face fear when others will not, while loyally protecting others. An athlete with THE HEART OF A LION leads its PRIDE with focused determination, diligence, and fierceness to the task at hand. Throughout the year, the Athletic Department will be focusing on this theme with the intent of being able to come alongside each student-athlete as they become more aware and intentional about practicing these character traits in themselves while displaying them for the FA Family, OUR LION PRIDE.
Life at Foundation Academy
At Foundation Academy we have methodically built a culture that takes into consideration growth and development from childhood to young adulthood. Our goal is to provide a broad, vibrant range of experiences that are a testament to who they are as individuals and how their gifts, talents, and passions fit into God’s perfect will for their lives. 
Our Tilden Campus sits on 63+ beautiful acres and is comprised of one main building called the Campus Life Center, our Technology & Math Annex, the double-sized Lieferman Gymnasium, and 4 small buildings dedicated to such disciplines as Band, Drama, and a few select other offerings. 
Hear It from Our HIGH school students
“I have been at Foundation Academy for four years and feel very blessed to be here. I have seen many wonderful changes over the past four years and the administration is excellent at what they do. I feel confident this will never waiver from its strong Christian beliefs.”
Anna Grace Williams, Class of 2019
Our Successes
Foundation Academy’s graduating classes have 100% college placement since 2010 and have been offered $3.4 million in scholarship opportunities last year alone.
Helpful Links
Ready to Tour Our School?
Foundation Academy has three incredible campuses to explore—all centered around quality Christian education. Schedule a tour today to get started.
Want to Learn More About FA?
At Foundation Academy, we have a lot to share. Request more information today to get the full scope of what our school has to offer.
markdown:
# Foundation Academy High School | Private Christian School

![Upper School Cover scaled](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/07/Upper-School-Cover-scaled.jpg)

## **Academics**

The upper school academic program at Foundation Academy is structured for students to earn a college-preparatory diploma in an engaging educational environment. The curriculum is designed to develop students who can reason, research, express, and critically think in order to prepare them for collegiate rigor.  The faculty consists of committed Christians who are experienced and passionate about their subject area and teach with a Biblical worldview.

Foundation Academy offers a variety of options for accelerated coursework at the high school level.  Students may participate in Honors classes, Advanced Placement (AP) courses, and Dual Enrollment (DE) courses through Southeastern University.  Participation in AP and DE courses allow students the opportunity to begin earning their first college credits while still in high school.  Foundation Academy also partners with Orange Technical College for students interested in pursuing trade-related careers.  Elective offerings consist of the fine arts, foreign languages, and technology areas.

![Teenagers in classroom](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/Teenagers-in-classroom-1014x1024.jpg)

![FA band](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/FA-band-995x1024.jpg)

## ****Extracurriculars****

Foundation Academy is continuously adding to what we refer to as “FA After 3”, after school programs, and extracurricular activities. Each one meant to complement the Foundation Academy experience and intended to engage each student’s passions and interests.

Upper School students participate in dozens of clubs and student organizations. Some clubs are chapters of national organizations or activities. Other clubs are just for student enjoyment or provide a service to the community or school.

## **Athletics**

It is the desire for Foundation Academy athletics to offer athletes a program that provides a variety of sports with godly coaches who are experts in their field. Coaches that understand the importance of developing fierce competitors in the playing arena, as well as in the Kingdom of God.

At Foundation Academy, student-athletes are pushed to develop what is referred to as **THE HEART OF A LION**, a bold heart that confronts adversity with strength, dignity, and wisdom; desiring to face fear when others will not, while loyally protecting others. An athlete with **THE HEART OF A LION** leads its **PRIDE** with focused determination, diligence, and fierceness to the task at hand. Throughout the year, the Athletic Department will be focusing on this theme with the intent of being able to come alongside each student-athlete as they become more aware and intentional about practicing these character traits in themselves while displaying them for the FA Family, **OUR LION PRIDE**.

![Varisty Football](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/07/Varisty-Football-1024x1006.jpg)

![FA High Schoolers](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/FA-High-Schoolers-1024x841.jpg)

## ****Life**** at Foundation Academy

At Foundation Academy we have methodically built a culture that takes into consideration growth and development from childhood to young adulthood. Our goal is to provide a broad, vibrant range of experiences that are a testament to who they are as individuals and how their gifts, talents, and passions fit into God’s perfect will for their lives.

Our Tilden Campus sits on 63+ beautiful acres and is comprised of one main building called the Campus Life Center, our Technology & Math Annex, the double-sized Lieferman Gymnasium, and 4 small buildings dedicated to such disciplines as Band, Drama, and a few select other offerings.

![Campuses Tilden Campus](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/Campuses_Tilden_Campus.jpg)

#### Hear It from Our HIGH school students

> “I have been at Foundation Academy for four years and feel very blessed to be here. I have seen many wonderful changes over the past four years and the administration is excellent at what they do. I feel confident this will never waiver from its strong Christian beliefs.”
> 
> Anna Grace Williams, Class of 2019

## Our **Successes**

Foundation Academy’s graduating classes have _100% college placement_ since 2010 and have been offered _$3.4 million in scholarship_ opportunities last year alone.

## Helpful **Links**

![Home News 1](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/Home_News_1.jpg)

## Ready to Tour Our School?

Foundation Academy has three incredible campuses to explore—all centered around quality Christian education. Schedule a tour today to get started.

![Home News 2 e1589483857905](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/Home_News_2-e1589483857905.jpg)

## Want to Learn More About FA?

At Foundation Academy, we have a lot to share. Request more information today to get the full scope of what our school has to offer.

openGraph.content: Foundation Academy Digital Arts Program: Explore your creativity with Adobe Photoshop and other digital art tools. Learn from experienced instructors and create stunning works of art that will amaze you.

text:
Digital Arts | Foundation Academy
Skip to content
The Foundation Academy Digital Arts Program’s primary focus is Adobe Photoshop. While our primary digital art tool is the computer, the class uses conventional methods in creation, such as color theory, texture, form, and shape. Merging traditional and modern means of creation inspires creativity and doesn’t limit our students in using their minds in the process. 
Upper School (7-12)
Using MAC Workstations students are being encouraged to explore original concepts using conventional methods of creation such as sketching and drawing. From there they are learning how to take their creation and produce it in the digital world. 
Our Tilden Campus has two MAC Labs used exclusively for the digital arts. It is set-up to encourage creativity, team work and collaborative learning all the while being creative. 
Program Highlights
While creativity can’t be taught, a person’s uniqueness can be cultivated in unique and unforeseen ways to be expressed visually in ways others can enjoy. Having the tools and the skill to use them can be taught and that’s is the goal of our Digital Arts Program. 
Apple History
Ironically students have very little prior knowledge of a world without computers and just what an impact it has had not only on our daily life but in particular the creative industries. So as a part of our Digitial Arts curriculum, students will learn the history of the computer and of one of the most iconic brands in the world, Apple. 
Toolbox Exploration 
As with any new toolbox, it must be filled with valuable tools. In digital arts our students will explore and learn the fundamentals elements of each tool and how they impact their ability to create. The possibilities in the mastery of these tools to create are endless!
Logo Design
It seems like no matter where you look we are inundated with symbols, marks and logos that help provide people, companies and organizations with their own unique identity. Our Digital Art students will explore the world of logo design by creating their very own a just one of their many lessons. 
Plenty of Great Music!
Digital Arts while based in technology is still a creaive endevour and takes place in a team enviorment that fosters creativity. In order to stoke some of that creative fire we have plently of cool vibes with some great music! 
Digital Arts Faculty

# Digital Arts | Foundation Academy

[Skip to content](https://www.foundationacademy.net/student-life/arts/digital-arts/#primary)

![Digital Arts 03 scaled](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/07/Digital-Arts-03-scaled.jpg)

The Foundation Academy Digital Arts Program’s primary focus is Adobe Photoshop. While our primary digital art tool is the computer, the class uses conventional methods in creation, such as color theory, texture, form, and shape. Merging traditional and modern means of creation inspires creativity and doesn’t limit our students in using their minds in the process.

## U**pper** School (7-12)

Using MAC Workstations students are being encouraged to explore original concepts using conventional methods of creation such as sketching and drawing. From there they are learning how to take their creation and produce it in the digital world.

Our Tilden Campus has two MAC Labs used exclusively for the digital arts. It is set-up to encourage creativity, team work and collaborative learning all the while being creative.

![Digital Art](https://www.foundationacademy.net/wp-content/uploads/2020/07/Digital-Art-1024x683.jpg)

## Program **Highlights**

While creativity can’t be taught, a person’s uniqueness can be cultivated in unique and unforeseen ways to be expressed visually in ways others can enjoy. Having the tools and the skill to use them can be taught and that’s is the goal of our Digital Arts Program.

### Apple History

Ironically students have very little prior knowledge of a world without computers and just what an impact it has had not only on our daily life but in particular the creative industries. So as a part of our Digitial Arts curriculum, students will learn the history of the computer and of one of the most iconic brands in the world, Apple.

### Toolbox Exploration

As with any new toolbox, it must be filled with valuable tools. In digital arts our students will explore and learn the fundamentals elements of each tool and how they impact their ability to create. The possibilities in the mastery of these tools to create are endless!

### Logo Design

It seems like no matter where you look we are inundated with symbols, marks and logos that help provide people, companies and organizations with their own unique identity. Our Digital Art students will explore the world of logo design by creating their very own a just one of their many lessons.

### Plenty of Great Music!

Digital Arts while based in technology is still a creaive endevour and takes place in a team enviorment that fosters creativity. In order to stoke some of that creative fire we have plently of cool vibes with some great music!

metadata.title: Multimedia & TV | Foundation Academy
metadata.description: Foundation Academy's Multimedia & TV program teaches students the skills they need to succeed in the creative industries. Students learn how to use video editing software, create animations, and produce live broadcasts. They also have the opportunity to work on real-world projects, such as creating videos for school events or producing a news program.

openGraph.content: Foundation Academy's Multimedia & TV program teaches students the skills they need to succeed in the creative industries. Students learn how to use video editing software, create animations, and produce live broadcasts. They also have the opportunity to work on real-world projects, such as creating videos for school events or producing a news program.

text:
Multimedia & TV | Foundation Academy
Skip to content
We desire to provide the creative and technical foundation to become a positive influence in the area of media and entertainment. The skills learned in this class will be helpful in creating projects for other classes as well. Students will be encouraged to use the talents they have been given to glorify God by seeking to make choices that are pleasing to God. Currently technology now allows us to provide cutting edge equipment to enhance the learning experience while preparing our students to develop valuable communication skills.
Lower School Multimedia
Our Foundation Academy Multimedia Program embraces the use of multiple streams of media such as text, photo, video, audio, graphics {non-text}, and animation to produce a wide range of student-centered projects and designs. With technology being the heart of our program, our students complete their assignments on their assigned MacBooks to design their masterpieces. Students are inspired to engage in critical thinking while using verbal and nonverbal methods which are used to promote a higher level of thinking while having fun with their peers.
Multimedia Foundations (7-12)
The purpose of this course is to provide a hands-on overview of MAC computers including various multimedia programs. The course also encourages expression through creativity with students creating projects while exploring the various programs. Students will also learn about our School News Show skills and may have an opportunity to participate.
TV Production I (7-12)
Students will learn basic skills in show production and editing. Projects will include a PSA, Commercial, Claymation, and a Silent B/W Video. They will also learn to identify possible segments and produce basic segments with a clear message for our school news show. Students will further develop production skills through more complex editing and will learn to create their own original music to be used in their segments.
Multimedia & TV Opportunities
Program Highlights
Students will demonstrate awareness of MAC computer systems, the impact of technology on careers and society and the need for its ethical use, and personal and occupational uses of computers and other technology. They will utilize various computer applications to manipulate images, exercise social and ethical responsibility in regards to current issues and biblical principles, and apply technology productivity tools to enhance learning and promote creativity.
Students will learn the history of television, practice basic video camera operation using various cameras, and demonstrate introductory post-production skills in graphics, audio, and editing using MAC computers. Students will also have the opportunity to learn basic scriptwriting and storyboarding skills alongside basic direction and production skills through video projects that will allow them to understand career opportunities in the television industry.
Become Proficient on MAC OS

Students will develop a strong understanding of the MAC OS environment, learning how to navigate, manage files, and utilize system tools to streamline their creative projects. They will gain essential skills in operating a MAC system, preparing them for professional use in multimedia and TV production.
Adobe Creative Suite Media Tools

Students will explore the full range of Adobe Creative Suite applications, from Photoshop to Premiere Pro, enabling them to create high-quality visuals, edit video content, and produce professional-grade multimedia projects. These tools are integral to mastering digital media in today’s creative industries.
Combine Conventional & Non-Conventional Methods

Students will learn to blend traditional video production techniques with cutting-edge digital methods, fostering innovation in their creative process. By integrating both conventional and non-conventional approaches, they will expand their problem-solving skills and explore new ways to tell compelling stories.
Learn to Master Hardware

Students will become proficient in handling video production equipment, including cameras, lighting, and audio devices. Through hands-on experience, they will gain confidence in setting up and using hardware to bring their creative vision to life, preparing them for real-world production scenarios.
Multimedia & TV Faculty
Student Testimonials
“Multimedia at Foundation Academy allowed me the opportunity to explore my creativity, express my ideas, and use both traditional and non-traditional tools to bring ideas to life. I would recommend the program highly! ”
Dom Zagame, Senior Media Student, Class of 2021

Students will demonstrate awareness of MAC computer systems, the impact of technology on careers and society and the need for its ethical use, and personal and occupational uses of computers and other technology. They will utilize various computer applications to manipulate images, exercise social and ethical responsibility in regards to current issues and biblical principles, and apply technology productivity tools to enhance learning and promote creativity.

Students will learn the history of television, practice basic video camera operation using various cameras, and demonstrate introductory post-production skills in graphics, audio, and editing using MAC computers. Students will also have the opportunity to learn basic scriptwriting and storyboarding skills alongside basic direction and production skills through video projects that will allow them to understand career opportunities in the television industry.

### Become Proficient on MAC OS

  
Students will develop a strong understanding of the MAC OS environment, learning how to navigate, manage files, and utilize system tools to streamline their creative projects. They will gain essential skills in operating a MAC system, preparing them for professional use in multimedia and TV production.

### Adobe Creative Suite Media Tools

  
Students will explore the full range of Adobe Creative Suite applications, from Photoshop to Premiere Pro, enabling them to create high-quality visuals, edit video content, and produce professional-grade multimedia projects. These tools are integral to mastering digital media in today’s creative industries.

### Combine Conventional & Non-Conventional Methods

  
Students will learn to blend traditional video production techniques with cutting-edge digital methods, fostering innovation in their creative process. By integrating both conventional and non-conventional approaches, they will expand their problem-solving skills and explore new ways to tell compelling stories.

### Learn to Master Hardware

  
Students will become proficient in handling video production equipment, including cameras, lighting, and audio devices. Through hands-on experience, they will gain confidence in setting up and using hardware to bring their creative vision to life, preparing them for real-world production scenarios.
